{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_785c82d99013481cb405f9bc","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Peer monitoring — stale claim release and crash cleanup.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Detects crashed or stuck workers by reading heartbeat files and comparing\n4\t//! them against the configured TTL. For crashed workers (stale heartbeat +\n5\t//! dead PID), releases the claimed bead, removes the heartbeat file, and\n6\t//! deregisters from the worker registry.\n7\t//!\n8\t//! This module is designed to be called by the Mend strand (Strand 2). No\n9\t//! other strand should perform peer monitoring.\n10\t//!\n11\t//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `health`, `registry`, `telemetry`, `types`.\n12\t\n13\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n14\tuse std::time::Duration;\n15\t\n16\tuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\n17\tuse chrono::Utc;\n18\t\n19\tuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\n20\tuse crate::health::{HealthMonitor, StalePeer};\n21\tuse crate::registry::Registry;\n22\tuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\n23\t\n24\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n25\t// PeerCheckResult\n26\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n27\t\n28\t/// Summary of a peer monitoring check cycle.\n29\t#[derive(Debug)]\n30\tpub struct PeerCheckResult {\n31\t    /// Number of crashed workers detected (dead PID).\n32\t    pub crashed_count: u32,\n33\t    /// Number of stuck workers detected (alive PID, stale heartbeat).\n34\t    pub stuck_count: u32,\n35\t    /// Number of beads released from crashed workers.\n36\t    pub beads_released: u32,\n37\t}\n38\t\n39\timpl PeerCheckResult {\n40\t    /// Whether any cleanup work was performed.\n41\t    pub fn did_work(&self) -> bool {\n42\t        self.beads_released > 0\n43\t    }\n44\t}\n45\t\n46\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n47\t// PeerMonitor\n48\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n49\t\n50\t/// Monitors peer workers for crashes and stale heartbeats.\n51\t///\n52\t/// Constructed with references to shared infrastructure (bead store, registry,\n53\t/// telemetry). Call `check_peers()` to run one monitoring cycle.\n54\tpub struct PeerMonitor<'a> {\n55\t    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n56\t    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n57\t    own_worker_id: String,\n58\t    store: &'a dyn BeadStore,\n59\t    registry: &'a Registry,\n60\t    telemetry: Telemetry,\n61\t}\n62\t\n63\timpl<'a> PeerMonitor<'a> {\n64\t    /// Create a new peer monitor.\n65\t    ///\n66\t    /// - `heartbeat_dir`: path to `~/.needle/state/heartbeats/`\n67\t    /// - `heartbeat_ttl`: how long before a heartbeat is considered stale\n68\t    /// - `own_worker_id`: this worker's ID (excluded from peer checks)\n69\t    /// - `store`: bead store for releasing orphaned beads\n70\t    /// - `registry`: worker registry for deregistering crashed workers\n71\t    /// - `telemetry`: telemetry emitter for peer events\n72\t    pub fn new(\n73\t        heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n74\t        heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n75\t        own_worker_id: String,\n76\t        store: &'a dyn BeadStore,\n77\t        registry: &'a Registry,\n78\t        telemetry: Telemetry,\n79\t    ) -> Self {\n80\t        PeerMonitor {\n81\t            heartbeat_dir,\n82\t            heartbeat_ttl,\n83\t            own_worker_id,\n84\t            store,\n85\t            registry,\n86\t            telemetry,\n87\t        }\n88\t    }\n89\t\n90\t    /// Run one peer monitoring cycle.\n91\t    ///\n92\t    /// Algorithm:\n93\t    /// 1. Read all heartbeat files\n94\t    /// 2. For each stale peer (excluding ourselves):\n95\t    ///    a. If PID is dead → CRASHED: release bead, remove heartbeat, deregister\n96\t    ///    b. If PID is alive → STUCK: emit warning only, do NOT release\n97\t    /// 3. Return summary of actions taken\n98\t    pub async fn check_peers(&self) -> Result<PeerCheckResult> {\n99\t        let heartbeats = HealthMonitor::read_all_heartbeats(&self.heartbeat_dir)?;\n100\t\n101\t        let mut result = PeerCheckResult {\n102\t            crashed_count: 0,\n103\t            stuck_count: 0,\n104\t            beads_released: 0,\n105\t        };\n106\t\n107\t        for hb in &heartbeats {\n108\t            // Skip our own heartbeat (match by qualified identity).\n109\t            if hb.qualified_id == self.own_worker_id {\n110\t                continue;\n111\t            }\n112\t\n113\t            // Only check stale heartbeats.\n114\t            if !HealthMonitor::is_stale(hb, self.heartbeat_ttl) {\n115\t                continue;\n116\t            }\n117\t\n118\t            let pid_alive = HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(hb.pid);\n119\t            let stale_peer = StalePeer {\n120\t                worker_id: hb.worker_id.clone(),\n121\t                qualified_id: Some(hb.qualified_id.clone()),\n122\t                pid: hb.pid,\n123\t                pid_alive,\n124\t                current_bead: hb.current_bead.clone(),\n125\t                last_heartbeat: hb.last_heartbeat,\n126\t                heartbeat_file: hb.heartbeat_file.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n127\t                    self.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", hb.qualified_id))\n128\t                }),\n129\t            };\n130\t\n131\t            if pid_alive {\n132\t                // STUCK: alive PID, stale heartbeat. Warn only — do NOT release.\n133\t                self.handle_stuck_peer(&stale_peer)?;\n134\t                result.stuck_count += 1;\n135\t            } else {\n136\t                // CRASHED: dead PID. Clean up.\n137\t                let released = self.handle_crashed_peer(&stale_peer).await?;\n138\t                result.crashed_count += 1;\n139\t                if released {\n140\t                    result.beads_released += 1;\n141\t                }\n142\t            }\n143\t        }\n144\t\n145\t        if result.crashed_count > 0 || result.stuck_count > 0 {\n146\t            tracing::info!(\n147\t                crashed = result.crashed_count,\n148\t                stuck = result.stuck_count,\n149\t                beads_released = result.beads_released,\n150\t                \"peer monitoring cycle complete\"\n151\t            );\n152\t        }\n153\t\n154\t        Ok(result)\n155\t    }\n156\t\n157\t    /// Handle a stuck peer: alive PID but stale heartbeat.\n158\t    ///\n159\t    /// Safety: never release the bead, never kill the process. Alert only.\n160\t    fn handle_stuck_peer(&self, peer: &StalePeer) -> Result<()> {\n161\t        let age_secs = Utc::now()\n162\t            .signed_duration_since(peer.last_heartbeat)\n163\t            .num_seconds()\n164\t            .max(0) as u64;\n165\t\n166\t        tracing::warn!(\n167\t            worker = %peer.worker_id,\n168\t            pid = peer.pid,\n169\t            age_secs,\n170\t            bead = ?peer.current_bead,\n171\t            \"peer is stuck (alive PID, stale heartbeat) — not releasing\"\n172\t        );\n173\t\n174\t        // Emit peer.stale telemetry for each stuck peer.\n175\t        if let Some(ref bead_id) = peer.current_bead {\n176\t            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckDetected {\n177\t                bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n178\t                age_secs,\n179\t            })?;\n180\t        }\n181\t\n182\t        Ok(())\n183\t    }\n184\t\n185\t    /// Handle a crashed peer: dead PID, stale heartbeat.\n186\t    ///\n187\t    /// 1. Release the claimed bead (if any)\n188\t    /// 2. Remove the heartbeat file\n189\t    /// 3. Deregister from the worker registry\n190\t    /// 4. Emit peer.crashed telemetry\n191\t    ///\n192\t    /// Returns `true` if a bead was released.\n193\t    async fn handle_crashed_peer(&self, peer: &StalePeer) -> Result<bool> {\n194\t        tracing::info!(\n195\t            worker = %peer.worker_id,\n196\t            pid = peer.pid,\n197\t            bead = ?peer.current_bead,\n198\t            \"peer crashed — cleaning up\"\n199\t        );\n200\t\n201\t        let mut bead_released = false;\n202\t\n203\t        // 1. Release the claimed bead (if any).\n204\t        if let Some(ref bead_id) = peer.current_bead {\n205\t            match self.store.release(bead_id).await {\n206\t                Ok(()) => {\n207\t                    tracing::info!(\n208\t                        bead = %bead_id,\n209\t                        worker = %peer.worker_id,\n210\t                        \"released orphaned bead from crashed worker\"\n211\t                    );\n212\t                    bead_released = true;\n213\t                }\n214\t                Err(e) => {\n215\t                    // Release failure is non-fatal — the bead may have already\n216\t                    // been released or closed by another path.\n217\t                    tracing::warn!(\n218\t                        bead = %bead_id,\n219\t                        worker = %peer.worker_id,\n220\t                        error = %e,\n221\t                        \"failed to release bead from crashed worker (may already be released)\"\n222\t                    );\n223\t                }\n224\t            }\n225\t\n226\t            // Emit peer.crashed telemetry.\n227\t            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n228\t                bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n229\t                peer_worker: peer\n230\t                    .qualified_id\n231\t                    .as_deref()\n232\t                    .unwrap_or(&peer.worker_id)\n233\t                    .to_string(),\n234\t            })?;\n235\t        }\n236\t\n237\t        // 2. Remove the heartbeat file.\n238\t        remove_heartbeat_file(&peer.heartbeat_file)?;\n239\t\n240\t        // 3. Deregister from the worker registry.\n241\t        let dereg_id = peer.qualified_id.as_deref().unwrap_or(&peer.worker_id);\n242\t        if let Err(e) = self.registry.deregister(dereg_id) {\n243\t            tracing::warn!(\n244\t                worker = %peer.worker_id,\n245\t                error = %e,\n246\t                \"failed to deregister crashed worker from registry\"\n247\t            );\n248\t        }\n249\t\n250\t        Ok(bead_released)\n251\t    }\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n255\t// Helpers\n256\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n257\t\n258\t/// Remove a heartbeat file (best-effort).\n259\tfn remove_heartbeat_file(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n260\t    match std::fs::remove_file(path) {\n261\t        Ok(()) => {\n262\t            tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"removed stale heartbeat file\");\n263\t            Ok(())\n264\t        }\n265\t        Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => {\n266\t            // Already gone — not an error.\n267\t            tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"heartbeat file already removed\");\n268\t            Ok(())\n269\t        }\n270\t        Err(e) => {\n271\t            Err(e).with_context(|| format!(\"failed to remove heartbeat file: {}\", path.display()))\n272\t        }\n273\t    }\n274\t}\n275\t\n276\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n277\t// Tests\n278\t//    fn write_heartbeat(dir: &Path, data: &HeartbeatData) {\n371\t        let name = if data.qualified_id.is_empty() {\n372\t            &data.worker_id\n373\t        } else {\n374\t            &data.qualified_id\n375\t        };\n376\t        let path = dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", name));\n377\t        let json = serde_json::to_string(data).unwrap();\n378\t        std::fs::write(path,     } else {\n391\t            Utc::now()\n392\t        };\n393\t\n394\t        HeartbeatData {\n395\t            worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n396\t            qualified_id: session: worker_id.to_string(),\n405\t            heartbeat_file: None,\n406\t        }\n407\t    }\n408\t\n409\t    #[tokio::test]\n410\t    async fn healthy_peers_are_ignored() {\n411\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n412\t        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n413\t        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n414\t\n415\t        // Write a fresh (non-stale) heartbeat for );\n420\t\n421\t        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new();\n422\t        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n423\t        let telemetry =    assert!(!result.did_work());\n439\t        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 0);\n440\t    }\n441\t\n442\t    #[tokio::test]\n443\t    async fn crashed_peer_bead_released() {\n444\t      stuck_count: 1, // stuck doesn't count as \"work\"\n724\t            beads_released: 0,\n725\t        };\n726\t        assert!(!no_work.did_work());\n727\t\n728\t        let did_work = PeerCheckResult {\n729\t            crashed_count: 1,\n730\t            stuck_count: 0,\n731\t            beads_released: 1,\n732\t        };\n733\t        assert!(did_work.did_work());\n734\t    }\n735\t}\n736\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"93d29db8-cd8d-4349-b3e0-4d5deb562d0d","uuid":"a5bd6173-2838-4fee-ba9a-8c1c9602825d","timestamp":"2026-04-26T00:36:32.297Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/peer/mod.rs","content":"//! Peer monitoring — stale claim release and crash cleanup.\n//!\n//! Detects crashed or stuck workers by reading heartbeat files and comparing\n//! them against the configured TTL. For crashed workers (stale heartbeat +\n//! dead PID), releases the claimed bead, removes the heartbeat file, and\n//! deregisters from the worker registry.\n//!\n//! This module is designed to be called by the Mend strand (Strand 2). No\n//! other strand should perform peer monitoring.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `health`, `registry`, `telemetry`, `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::health::{HealthMonitor, StalePeer};\nuse crate::registry::Registry;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PeerCheckResult\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Summary of a peer monitoring check cycle.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct PeerCheckResult {\n    /// Number of crashed workers detected (dead PID).\n    pub crashed_count: u32,\n    /// Number of stuck workers detected (alive PID, stale heartbeat).\n    pub stuck_count: u32,\n    /// Number of beads released from crashed workers.\n    pub beads_released: u32,\n}\n\nimpl PeerCheckResult {\n    /// Whether any cleanup work was performed.\n    pub fn did_work(&self) -> bool {\n        self.beads_released > 0\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PeerMonitor\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Monitors peer workers for crashes and stale heartbeats.\n///\n/// Constructed with references to shared infrastructure (bead store, registry,\n/// telemetry). Call `check_peers()` to run one monitoring cycle.\npub struct PeerMonitor<'a> {\n    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n    own_worker_id: String,\n    store: &'a dyn BeadStore,\n    registry: &'a Registry,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n}\n\nimpl<'a> PeerMonitor<'a> {\n    /// Create a new peer monitor.\n    ///\n    /// - `heartbeat_dir`: path to `~/.needle/state/heartbeats/`\n    /// - `heartbeat_ttl`: how long before a heartbeat is considered stale\n    /// - `own_worker_id`: this worker's ID (excluded from peer checks)\n    /// - `store`: bead store for releasing orphaned beads\n    /// - `registry`: worker registry for deregistering crashed workers\n    /// - `telemetry`: telemetry emitter for peer events\n    pub fn new(\n        heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n        heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n        own_worker_id: String,\n        store: &'a dyn BeadStore,\n        registry: &'a Registry,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n    ) -> Self {\n        PeerMonitor {\n            heartbeat_dir,\n            heartbeat_ttl,\n            own_worker_id,\n            store,\n            registry,\n            telemetry,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run one peer monitoring cycle.\n    ///\n    /// Algorithm:\n    /// 1. Read all heartbeat files\n    /// 2. For each stale peer (excluding ourselves):\n    ///    a. If PID is dead → CRASHED: release bead, remove heartbeat, deregister\n    ///    b. If PID is alive → STUCK: emit warning only, do NOT release\n    /// 3. Return summary of actions taken\n    pub async fn check_peers(&self) -> Result<PeerCheckResult> {\n        let heartbeats = HealthMonitor::read_all_heartbeats(&self.heartbeat_dir)?;\n\n        let mut result = PeerCheckResult {\n            crashed_count: 0,\n            stuck_count: 0,\n            beads_released: 0,\n        };\n\n        for hb in &heartbeats {\n            // Skip our own heartbeat (match by qualified identity).\n            if hb.qualified_id == self.own_worker_id {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Only check stale heartbeats.\n            if !HealthMonitor::is_stale(hb, self.heartbeat_ttl) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let pid_alive = HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(hb.pid);\n            let stale_peer = StalePeer {\n                worker_id: hb.worker_id.clone(),\n                qualified_id: Some(hb.qualified_id.clone()),\n                pid: hb.pid,\n                pid_alive,\n                current_bead: hb.current_bead.clone(),\n                last_heartbeat: hb.last_heartbeat,\n                heartbeat_file: hb.heartbeat_file.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                    self.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", hb.qualified_id))\n                }),\n            };\n\n            if pid_alive {\n                // STUCK: alive PID, stale heartbeat. Warn only — do NOT release.\n                self.handle_stuck_peer(&stale_peer)?;\n                result.stuck_count += 1;\n            } else {\n                // CRASHED: dead PID. Clean up.\n                let released = self.handle_crashed_peer(&stale_peer).await?;\n                result.crashed_count += 1;\n                if released {\n                    result.beads_released += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if result.crashed_count > 0 || result.stuck_count > 0 {\n            tracing::info!(\n                crashed = result.crashed_count,\n                stuck = result.stuck_count,\n                beads_released = result.beads_released,\n                \"peer monitoring cycle complete\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a stuck peer: alive PID but stale heartbeat.\n    ///\n    /// Safety: never release the bead, never kill the process. Alert only.\n    fn handle_stuck_peer(&self, peer: &StalePeer) -> Result<()> {\n        let age_secs = Utc::now()\n            .signed_duration_since(peer.last_heartbeat)\n            .num_seconds()\n            .max(0) as u64;\n\n        tracing::warn!(\n            worker = %peer.worker_id,\n            pid = peer.pid,\n            age_secs,\n            bead = ?peer.current_bead,\n            \"peer is stuck (alive PID, stale heartbeat) — not releasing\"\n        );\n\n        // Emit peer.stale telemetry for each stuck peer.\n        if let Some(ref bead_id) = peer.current_bead {\n            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckDetected {\n                bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                age_secs,\n            })?;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a crashed peer: dead PID, stale heartbeat.\n    ///\n    /// 1. Release the claimed bead (if any)\n    /// 2. Remove the heartbeat file\n    /// 3. Deregister from the worker registry\n    /// 4. Emit peer.crashed telemetry\n    ///\n    /// Returns `true` if a bead was released.\n    async fn handle_crashed_peer(&self, peer: &StalePeer) -> Result<bool> {\n        tracing::info!(\n            worker = %peer.worker_id,\n            pid = peer.pid,\n            bead = ?peer.current_bead,\n            \"peer crashed — cleaning up\"\n        );\n\n        let mut bead_released = false;\n\n        // 1. Release the claimed bead (if any).\n        if let Some(ref bead_id) = peer.current_bead {\n            match self.store.release(bead_id).await {\n                Ok(()) => {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        bead = %bead_id,\n                        worker = %peer.worker_id,\n                        \"released orphaned bead from crashed worker\"\n                    );\n                    bead_released = true;\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    // Release failure is non-fatal — the bead may have already\n                    // been released or closed by another path.\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        bead = %bead_id,\n                        worker = %peer.worker_id,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"failed to release bead from crashed worker (may already be released)\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Emit peer.crashed telemetry.\n            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n                bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                peer_worker: peer\n                    .qualified_id\n                    .as_deref()\n                    .unwrap_or(&peer.worker_id)\n                    .to_string(),\n            })?;\n        }\n\n        // 2. Remove the heartbeat file.\n        remove_heartbeat_file(&peer.heartbeat_file)?;\n\n        // 3. Deregister from the worker registry.\n        let dereg_id = peer.qualified_id.as_deref().unwrap_or(&peer.worker_id);\n        if let Err(e) = self.registry.deregister(dereg_id) {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                worker = %peer.worker_id,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to deregister crashed worker from registry\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(bead_released)\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Helpers\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Remove a heartbeat file (best-effort).\nfn remove_heartbeat_file(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n    match std::fs::remove_file(path) {\n        Ok(()) => {\n            tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"removed stale heartbeat file\");\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => {\n            // Already gone — not an error.\n            tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"heartbeat file already removed\");\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            Err(e).with_context(|| format!(\"failed to remove heartbeat file: {}\", path.display()))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Tests\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::{Filters, RepairReport};\n    use crate::health::HeartbeatData;\n    use crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, ClaimResult, WorkerState};\n    use async_trait::async_trait;\n    use chrono::Utc;\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\n    use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};\n    use std::sync::Arc;\n\n    // ── Mock bead store ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Track which beads were released.\n    struct MockBeadStore {\n        release_count: Arc<AtomicU32>,\n    }\n\n    impl MockBeadStore {\n        fn new() -> (Self, Arc<AtomicU32>) {\n            let count = Arc::new(AtomicU32::new(0));\n            (\n                MockBeadStore {\n                    release_count: count.clone(),\n                },\n                count,\n            )\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for MockBeadStore {\n        async fn ready(&self, _filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn show(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented in mock\")\n        }\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            anyhow::bail!(\"not implemented in mock\")\n        }\n        async fn release(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            self.release_count.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn reopen(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn labels(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn add_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn remove_label(&self, _id: &BeadId, _label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn create_bead(&self, _title: &str, _body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"mock-bead\"))\n        }\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<RepairReport> {\n            Ok(RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    fn write_heartbeat(dir: &Path, data: &HeartbeatData) {\n        let name = if data.qualified_id.is_empty() {\n            &data.worker_id\n        } else {\n            &data.qualified_id\n        };\n        let path = dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", name));\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(data).unwrap();\n        std::fs::write(path, json).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    fn make_heartbeat(\n        worker_id: &str,\n        pid: u32,\n        bead_id: Option<&str>,\n        stale: bool,\n    ) -> HeartbeatData {\n        let last_heartbeat = if stale {\n            // 10 minutes ago — well past any reasonable TTL\n            Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::seconds(600)\n        } else {\n            Utc::now()\n        };\n\n        HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: worker_id.to_string(),\n            qualified_id: format!(\"claude-{}\", worker_id),\n            pid,\n            state: WorkerState::Executing,\n            current_bead: bead_id.map(BeadId::from),\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            last_heartbeat,\n            started_at: Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::seconds(3600),\n            beads_processed: 0,\n            session: worker_id.to_string(),\n            heartbeat_file: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn healthy_peers_are_ignored() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Write a fresh (non-stale) heartbeat for another worker.\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"other-worker\", std::process::id(), Some(\"nd-abc\"), false),\n        );\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new(\"test-monitor\".to_string());\n\n        let monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            hb_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            \"my-worker\".to_string(),\n            &store,\n            &registry,\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let result = monitor.check_peers().await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.crashed_count, 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.stuck_count, 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.beads_released, 0);\n        assert!(!result.did_work());\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn crashed_peer_bead_released() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Write a stale heartbeat with a dead PID (99999999).\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"dead-worker\", 99_999_999, Some(\"nd-orphan\"), true),\n        );\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new(\"test-monitor\".to_string());\n\n        let monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            hb_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            \"my-worker\".to_string(),\n            &store,\n            &registry,\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let result = monitor.check_peers().await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.crashed_count, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.stuck_count, 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.beads_released, 1);\n        assert!(result.did_work());\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 1);\n\n        // Heartbeat file should be removed.\n        let hb_path = hb_dir.join(\"claude-dead-worker.json\");\n        assert!(!hb_path.exists(), \"heartbeat file should be removed\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn stuck_peer_not_released() {\n      // Use a different worker_id so it's not skipped as \"self\".\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"stuck-worker\", std::process::id(), Some(\"nd-busy\"), assert_eq!(result.crashed_count, 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.stuck_count, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.beads_released, 0);\n        assert!(!result.did_work());\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 0);\n\n        // Heartbeat file should NOT be removed for stuck workers.\n        let hb_path = hb_dir.join(\"claude-stuck-worker.json\");\n multiple_peers_mixed_states() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path();\n        let reg_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Healthy peer (fresh heartbeat).\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"healthy\", std::process::id(), Some(\"nd-1\"), false),\n        );\n        // Stuck peer (stale heartbeat, dead PID).\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"crashed\", 99_999_999, Some(\"nd-3\"), true),\n        );\n        // Our own heartbeat (should be skipped even if stale).\n        write_heartbeat(\n            hb_dir,\n            &make_heartbeat(\"my-worker\", std::process::id(), Some(\"nd-4\"), true),\n        );\n\n        let (store, release_count) = MockBeadStore::new();\n        let registry = Registry::new(reg_dir.path());\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::new(\"test-monitor\".to_string());\n\n        let monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            hb_dir.to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            \"claude-my-worker\".to_string(),\n            &store,\n            &registry,\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let result = monitor.check_peers().await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.crashed_count, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.stuck_count, 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.beads_released, 1);\n        assert!(result.did_work());\n        assert_eq!(release_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed), 1);\n\n        // Only the crashed peer's heartbeat should be serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n18\t\n19\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n20\t// PID liveness checking (platform-specific)\n21\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n22\t\n23\t/// Check if a process with the given PID is currently running.\n24\t///\n25\t/// Returns `false` if the PID does not exist or if we lack permission to signal it.\n26\t/// This is best-effort: if we can't determine liveness, we assume the process is dead\n27\t/// to avoid counting stale entries toward concurrency limits.\n28\tpub fn is_pid_alive(pid: u32) -> bool {\n29\t    #[cfg(unix)]\n30\t    {\n31\t        // Use libc::kill with signal 0 to check if process exists.\n32\t        // SAFETY: kill(pid, 0) only checks existence, doesn't send a signal.\n33\t        unsafe {\n34\t            let ret = libc::kill(pid as i32, 0);\n35\t            if ret == 0 {\n36\t                // kill succeeded: process exists and we have permission to signal it.\n37\t                return true;\n38\t            }\n39\t            // kill failed: check errno to distinguish EPERM from ESRCH.\n40\t            // We must read errno immediately after kill, before any other syscalls.\n41\t            let errno = *libc::__errno_location();\n42\t            match errno {\n43\t                libc::ESRCH => {\n44\t                    // No such process: PID is dead.\n45\t                    false\n46\t                }\n47\t                libc::EPERM => {\n48\t                    // Process exists but we don't have permission to signal it.\n49\t                    // We treat this as \"alive\" since the process actually exists.\n50\t                    true\n51\t                }\n52\t                _ => {\n53\t                    // Other errors (e.g., EINVAL for invalid signal).\n54\t                    // Conservatively treat as dead to avoid false positives.\n55\t                    false\n56\t                }\n57\t            }\n58\t        }\n59\t    }\n60\t\n61\t    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n62\t    {\n63\t        // On non-Unix platforms, conservatively return false (assume dead).\n64\t        // This prevents false positives where dead PIDs are counted as live.\n65\t        // TODO: Implement Windows liveness check via OpenProcess if needed.\n66\t        false\n67\t    }\n68\t}\n69\t\n70\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n71\t// WorkerEntry\n72\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n73\t\n74\t/// A single worker entry in the registry.\n75\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]\n76\tpub struct WorkerEntry {\n77\t    /// Fully-qualified worker identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`, e.g., `claude-foxtrot`).\n78\t    pub id: String,\n79\t    /// Process ID of the worker.\n80\t    pub pid: u32,\n81\t    /// Workspace the worker is processing beads from.\n82\t    pub workspace: PathBuf,\n83\t    /// Agent adapter name.\n84\t    pub agent: String,\n85\t    /// Model name (if known).\n86\t    pub model: Option<String>,\n87\t    /// Provider name (e.g., `anthropic`, `openai`).\n88\t    pub provider: Option<String>,\n89\t    /// When the worker started.\n90\t    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n91\t    /// Number of beads processed so far.\n92\t    pub beads_processed: u64,\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n96\t// RegistryFile\n97\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n98\t\n99\t/// The on-disk JSON structure for `workers.json`.\n100\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n101\tpub struct RegistryFile {\n102\t    pub workers: Vec<WorkerEntry>,\n103\t    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n104\t}\n105\t\n106\timpl Default for RegistryFile {\n107\t    fn default() -> Self {\n108\t        RegistryFile {\n109\t            workers: Vec::new(),\n110\t            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n111\t        }\n112\t    }\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n116\t// Registry\n117\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n118\t\n119\t/// Worker state registry with flock-protected read-modify-write access.\n120\t#[derive(Clone)]\n121\tpub struct Registry {\n122\t    path: PathBuf,\n123\t}\n124\t\n125\timpl Registry {\n126\t    /// Create a registry instance pointing at the given state directory.\n127\t    ///\n128\t    /// The directory will be created on first write if it does not exist.\n129\t    pub fn new(state_dir: &Path) -> Self {\n130\t        Registry {\n131\t            path: state_dir.join(\"workers.json\"),\n132\t        }\n133\t    }\n134\t\n135\t    /// Create a registry using the default state directory (`~/.needle/state`).\n136\t    pub fn default_location(needle_home: &Path) -> Self {\n137\t        Self::new(&needle_home.join(\"state\"))\n138\t    }\n139\t\n140\t    /// Path to the workers.json file.\n141\t    pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {\n142\t        &self.path\n143\t    }\n144\t\n145\t    /// Register a worker on startup.\n146\t    ///\n147\t    /// If a worker with the same ID already exists, it is replaced (handles\n148\t    /// stale entries from previous runs).\n149\t    pub fn register(&self, entry: WorkerEntry) -> Result<()> {\n150\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n151\t            // Remove any existing entry with the same ID (stale from previous run).\n152\t            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != entry.id);\n153\t            reg.workers.push(entry);\n154\t        })\n155\t    }\n156\t\n157\t    /// Deregister a worker on shutdown.\n158\t    ///\n159\t    /// Best-effort: errors are logged but not propagated to avoid masking\n160\t    /// the real shutdown reason.\n161\t    pub fn deregister(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n162\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n163\t            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != worker_id);\n164\t        })\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Update a worker's beads_processed count.\n168\t    pub fn update_beads_processed(&self, worker_id: &str, beads_processed: u64) -> Result<()> {\n169\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n170\t            if let Some(entry) = reg.workers.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == worker_id) {\n171\t                entry.beads_processed = beads_processed;\n172\t            }\n173\t        })\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    /// Update a worker's current workspace.\n177\t    pub fn update_workspace(&self, worker_id: &str, workspace: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n178\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n179\t            if let Some(entry) = reg.workers.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == worker_id) {\n180\t                entry.workspace = workspace.to_path_buf();\n181\t            }\n182\t        })\n183\t    }\n184\t\n185\t    /// Read all registered workers, filtering out entries for dead PIDs.\n186\t    ///\n187\t    /// This lazy cleanup ensures that workers killed via SIGKILL or crashes\n188\t    /// don't accumulate in the registry and falsely count toward concurrency\n189\t    /// limits.\n190\t    pub fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<WorkerEntry>> {\n191\t        let reg = self.read()?;\n192\t        let total_count = reg.workers.len();\n193\t        let live_workers: Vec<WorkerEntry> = reg\n194\t            .workers\n195\t            .into_iter()\n196\t            .filter(|w| is_pid_alive(w.pid))\n197\t            .collect();\n198\t\n199\t        // If we filtered out dead entries, persist the cleanup so we don't\n200\t        // need to re-check their PIDs on every read. This is best-effort:\n201\t        // if the write fails (disk full, race with another writer), we still\n202\t        // return the correctly filtered list — we'll just re-filter next time.\n203\t        let live_count = live_workers.len();\n204\t        if live_count != total_count {\n205\t            let dead_count = total_count - live_count;\n206\t            tracing::debug!(dead_count, \"filtered dead worker entries from registry\");\n207\t\n208\t            let cleaned_reg = RegistryFile {\n209\t                workers: live_workers.clone(),\n210\t                updated_at: Utc::now(),\n211\t            };\n212\t            if let Err(e) = self.write_cleaned(cleaned_reg) {\n213\t                tracing::warn!(\n214\t                    error = %e,\n215\t                    \"failed to persist dead PID cleanup; will re-filter next read\"\n216\t                );\n217\t            }\n218\t        }\n219\t\n220\t        Ok(live_workers)\n221\t    }\n222\t\n223\t    /// Write a cleaned registry file (used by list() to persist dead PID filtering).\n224\t    ///\n225\t    /// No file locking: read() already released its shared lock before returning.\n226\t    /// The atomic rename ensures concurrent writers don't corrupt the file —\n227\t    /// one write wins cleanly.\n228\t    fn write_cleaned(&self, reg: RegistryFile) -> Result<()> {\n229\t        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n230\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n231\t        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n232\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n233\t        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n234\t            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n235\t        })?;\n236\t        Ok(())\n237\t    }\n238\t\n239\t    // ── Internal helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n240\t\n241\t    /// Read the registry file, returning a default if it doesn't exist.\n242\t    fn read(&self) -> Result<RegistryFile> {\n243\t        if !self.path.exists() {\n244\t            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n245\t        }\n246\t\n247\t        let file = std::fs::File::open(&self.path)\n248\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n249\t\n250\t        // Shared lock for reading (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n251\t        FileExt::lock_shared(&file)\n252\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to acquire shared lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n253\t\n254\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path)\n255\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n256\t\n257\t        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n258\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n259\t\n260\t        if content.trim().is_empty() {\n261\t            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n262\t        }\n263\t\n264\t        serde_json::from_str(&content)\n265\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to parse registry JSON: {}\", self.path.display()))\n266\t    }\n267\t\n268\t    /// Perform a flock-protected read-modify-write operation.\n269\t    fn modify<F>(&self, mutator: F) -> Result<()>\n270\t    where\n271\t        F: FnOnce(&mut RegistryFile),\n272\t    {\n273\t        // Ensure the parent directory exists.\n274\t        if let Some(parent) = self.path.parent() {\n275\t            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).with_context(|| {\n276\t                format!(\"failed to create registry directory: {}\", parent.display())\n277\t            })?;\n278\t        }\n279\t\n280\t        // Open or create the file.\n281\t        let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n282\t            .read(true)\n283\t            .write(true)\n284\t            .create(true)\n285\t            .truncate(false)\n286\t            .open(&self.path)\n287\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n288\t\n289\t        // Exclusive lock for writing (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n290\t        FileExt::lock_exclusive(&file).with_context(|| {\n291\t            format!(\"failed to acquire exclusive lock: {}\", self.path.display())\n292\t        })?;\n293\t\n294\t        // Read current contents.\n295\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path).unwrap_or_default();\n296\t        let mut reg: RegistryFile = if content.trim().is_empty() {\n297\t            RegistryFile::default()\n298\t        } else {\n299\t            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_default()\n300\t        };\n301\t\n302\t        // Apply the mutation.\n303\t        mutator(&mut reg);\n304\t        reg.updated_at = Utc::now();\n305\t\n306\t        // Write back atomically: write to temp file, then rename.\n307\t        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n308\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n309\t        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n310\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n311\t        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n312\t            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n313\t        })?;\n314\t\n315\t        // Release lock (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n316\t        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n317\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n318\t\n319\t        Ok(())\n320\t    }\n321\t}\n322\t\n323\t//   fn is_pid_alive_returns_true_for_current_process() {\n553\t        // The current process's PID should be alive.\n554\t        assert!(is_pid_alive(std::process::id()));\n555\t    }\n556\t\n557\t    #[test]\n558\t    fn is_pid_alive_returns_false_for_nonexistent_pid() {\n559\t        // A PID that's unlikely to exist should be dead.\n560\t        // DEAD_PID is well beyond any valid PID on real }\n563\t}\n564\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"93d29db8-cd8d-4349-b3e0-4d5deb562d0d","uuid":"f94e8b85-d538-4b9c-913a-d237634daea8","timestamp":"2026-04-26T00:36:44.845Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/registry/mod.rs","content":"//! Worker state registry — shared JSON file tracking all active workers.\n//!\n//! The registry is informational: \"who is running, what are they doing, how\n//! many beads processed.\" It is NOT used for coordination — heartbeats handle\n//! that.\n//!\n//! File: `~/.needle/state/workers.json`\n//! Access: flock-protected read-modify-write (atomic updates).\n//!\n//! Depends on: `config`, `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse fs2::FileExt;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PID liveness checking (platform-specific)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Check if a process with the given PID is currently running.\n///\n/// Returns `false` if the PID does not exist or if we lack permission to signal it.\n/// This is best-effort: if we can't determine liveness, we assume the process is dead\n/// to avoid counting stale entries toward concurrency limits.\npub fn is_pid_alive(pid: u32) -> bool {\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    {\n        // Use libc::kill with signal 0 to check if process exists.\n        // SAFETY: kill(pid, 0) only checks existence, doesn't send a signal.\n        unsafe {\n            let ret = libc::kill(pid as i32, 0);\n            if ret == 0 {\n                // kill succeeded: process exists and we have permission to signal it.\n                return true;\n            }\n            // kill failed: check errno to distinguish EPERM from ESRCH.\n            // We must read errno immediately after kill, before any other syscalls.\n            let errno = *libc::__errno_location();\n            match errno {\n                libc::ESRCH => {\n                    // No such process: PID is dead.\n                    false\n                }\n                libc::EPERM => {\n                    // Process exists but we don't have permission to signal it.\n                    // We treat this as \"alive\" since the process actually exists.\n                    true\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    // Other errors (e.g., EINVAL for invalid signal).\n                    // Conservatively treat as dead to avoid false positives.\n                    false\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    {\n        // On non-Unix platforms, conservatively return false (assume dead).\n        // This prevents false positives where dead PIDs are counted as live.\n        // TODO: Implement Windows liveness check via OpenProcess if needed.\n        false\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// WorkerEntry\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A single worker entry in the registry.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct WorkerEntry {\n    /// Fully-qualified worker identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`, e.g., `claude-foxtrot`).\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Process ID of the worker.\n    pub pid: u32,\n    /// Workspace the worker is processing beads from.\n    pub workspace: PathBuf,\n    /// Agent adapter name.\n    pub agent: String,\n    /// Model name (if known).\n    pub model: Option<String>,\n    /// Provider name (e.g., `anthropic`, `openai`).\n    pub provider: Option<String>,\n    /// When the worker started.\n    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Number of beads processed so far.\n    pub beads_processed: u64,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// RegistryFile\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// The on-disk JSON structure for `workers.json`.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RegistryFile {\n    pub workers: Vec<WorkerEntry>,\n    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for RegistryFile {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        RegistryFile {\n            workers: Vec::new(),\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Registry\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Worker state registry with flock-protected read-modify-write access.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct Registry {\n    path: PathBuf,\n}\n\nimpl Registry {\n    /// Create a registry instance pointing at the given state directory.\n    ///\n    /// The directory will be created on first write if it does not exist.\n    pub fn new(state_dir: &Path) -> Self {\n        Registry {\n            path: state_dir.join(\"workers.json\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a registry using the default state directory (`~/.needle/state`).\n    pub fn default_location(needle_home: &Path) -> Self {\n        Self::new(&needle_home.join(\"state\"))\n    }\n\n    /// Path to the workers.json file.\n    pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {\n        &self.path\n    }\n\n    /// Register a worker on startup.\n    ///\n    /// If a worker with the same ID already exists, it is replaced (handles\n    /// stale entries from previous runs).\n    pub fn register(&self, entry: WorkerEntry) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            // Remove any existing entry with the same ID (stale from previous run).\n            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != entry.id);\n            reg.workers.push(entry);\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Deregister a worker on shutdown.\n    ///\n    /// Best-effort: errors are logged but not propagated to avoid masking\n    /// the real shutdown reason.\n    pub fn deregister(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != worker_id);\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Update a worker's beads_processed count.\n    pub fn update_beads_processed(&self, worker_id: &str, beads_processed: u64) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            if let Some(entry) = reg.workers.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == worker_id) {\n                entry.beads_processed = beads_processed;\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Update a worker's current workspace.\n    pub fn update_workspace(&self, worker_id: &str, workspace: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            if let Some(entry) = reg.workers.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == worker_id) {\n                entry.workspace = workspace.to_path_buf();\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Read all registered workers, filtering out entries for dead PIDs.\n    ///\n    /// This lazy cleanup ensures that workers killed via SIGKILL or crashes\n    /// don't accumulate in the registry and falsely count toward concurrency\n    /// limits.\n    pub fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<WorkerEntry>> {\n        let reg = self.read()?;\n        let total_count = reg.workers.len();\n        let live_workers: Vec<WorkerEntry> = reg\n            .workers\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter(|w| is_pid_alive(w.pid))\n            .collect();\n\n        // If we filtered out dead entries, persist the cleanup so we don't\n        // need to re-check their PIDs on every read. This is best-effort:\n        // if the write fails (disk full, race with another writer), we still\n        // return the correctly filtered list — we'll just re-filter next time.\n        let live_count = live_workers.len();\n        if live_count != total_count {\n            let dead_count = total_count - live_count;\n            tracing::debug!(dead_count, \"filtered dead worker entries from registry\");\n\n            let cleaned_reg = RegistryFile {\n                workers: live_workers.clone(),\n                updated_at: Utc::now(),\n            };\n            if let Err(e) = self.write_cleaned(cleaned_reg) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to persist dead PID cleanup; will re-filter next read\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(live_workers)\n    }\n\n    /// Write a cleaned registry file (used by list() to persist dead PID filtering).\n    ///\n    /// No file locking: read() already released its shared lock before returning.\n    /// The atomic rename ensures concurrent writers don't corrupt the file —\n    /// one write wins cleanly.\n    fn write_cleaned(&self, reg: RegistryFile) -> Result<()> {\n        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n        })?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Internal helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Read the registry file, returning a default if it doesn't exist.\n    fn read(&self) -> Result<RegistryFile> {\n        if !self.path.exists() {\n            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n        }\n\n        let file = std::fs::File::open(&self.path)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        // Shared lock for reading (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n        FileExt::lock_shared(&file)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to acquire shared lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        if content.trim().is_empty() {\n            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n        }\n\n        serde_json::from_str(&content)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to parse registry JSON: {}\", self.path.display()))\n    }\n\n    /// Perform a flock-protected read-modify-write operation.\n    fn modify<F>(&self, mutator: F) -> Result<()>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&mut RegistryFile),\n    {\n        // Ensure the parent directory exists.\n        if let Some(parent) = self.path.parent() {\n            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).with_context(|| {\n                format!(\"failed to create registry directory: {}\", parent.display())\n            })?;\n        }\n\n        // Open or create the file.\n        let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n            .read(true)\n            .write(true)\n            .create(true)\n            .truncate(false)\n            .open(&self.path)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        // Exclusive lock for writing (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n        FileExt::lock_exclusive(&file).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\"failed to acquire exclusive lock: {}\", self.path.display())\n        })?;\n\n        // Read current contents.\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path).unwrap_or_default();\n        let mut reg: RegistryFile = if content.trim().is_empty() {\n            RegistryFile::default()\n        } else {\n            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_default()\n        };\n\n        // Apply the mutation.\n        mutator(&mut reg);\n        reg.updated_at = Utc::now();\n\n        // Write back atomically: write to temp file, then rename.\n        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n        })?;\n\n        // Release lock (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Tests\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn make_entry(id: &str) -> WorkerEntry {\n        WorkerEntry {\n            id: id.to_string(),\n            pid: std::process::id(),\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-workspace\"),\n            agent: \"claude\".to_string(),\n            model: Some(\"sonnet\".to_string()),\n            provider: Some(\"anthropic\".to_string()),\n            started_at: Utc::now(),\n            beads_processed: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// A PID that is definitely not in use on any real system.\n    ///\n    /// This must be a value that:\n    /// 1. Is positive when cast to i32 (avoid -1 special case)\n    /// 2. Is unlikely to ever be assigned by the kernel\n    ///\n    /// We use 9999999 which is well beyond typical PID ranges.\n    const DEAD_PID: u32 = 9999999;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn register_and_list() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"bravo\")).unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].id, \"alpha\");\n        assert_eq!(workers[1].id, \"bravo\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn deregister_removes_worker() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"bravo\")).unwrap();\n        reg.deregister(\"alpha\").unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].id, \"bravo\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn deregister_nonexistent_is_noop() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.deregister(\"nonexistent\").unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn register_replaces_stale_entry() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        let mut entry1 = make_entry(\"alpha\");\n        entry1.beads_processed = 5;\n        reg.register(entry1).unwrap();\n\n        let entry2 = make_entry(\"alpha\");\n        reg.register(entry2).unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].beads_processed, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn update_beads_processed() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.update_beads_processed(\"alpha\", 42).unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].beads_processed, 42);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn update_nonexistent_worker_is_noop() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.update_beads_processed(\"ghost\", 10).unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].beads_processed, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn empty_registry_returns_empty_list() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert!(workers.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_file_is_valid_json() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n   assert_eq!(parsed.workers.len(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn default_location_uses_state_subdir() {\n        let reg = Registry::default_location(Path::new(\"/home/test/.needle\"));\n        assert_eq!(\n            reg.path(),\n            Path::new(\"/home/test/.needle/state/workers.json\")\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn concurrent_registration_no_corruption() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Simulate sequential registrations (true concurrency tested in integration tests).\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n        for i in 0..10 {\n            reg.register(make_entry(&format!(\"worker-{i}\"))).unwrap();\n        }\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 10);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_json_roundtrip() {\n        let entry = make_entry(\"alpha\");\n        let reg_file = RegistryFile {\n            workers: vec![entry.clone()],\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        };\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg_file).unwrap();\n        let parsed: RegistryFile = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();\n entry.agent);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn list_filters_out_dead_pids() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        // Register a live worker (current process).\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"live-alpha\")).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an entry with a fake (dead) PID.\n        let mut dead_entry = make_entry(\"dead-worker\");\n        dead_entry.pid = DEAD_PID;\n\n        // Write the dead entry directly to the file.\n        let file_content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&RegistryFile {\n            workers: vec![make_entry(\"live-alpha\"), dead_entry],\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        })\n        .unwrap();\n        std::fs::write(reg.path(), file_content).unwrap();\n\n        // list() should filter out the dead PID.\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].id, \"live-alpha\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn DEAD_PID;\n\n        // Write both entries to the file.\n        let file_content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&RegistryFile {\n            workers: vec![make_entry(\"live-bravo\"), dead_entry],\n assert!(is_pid_alive(std::process::id()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn is_pid_alive_returns_false_for_nonexistent_pid() {\n        // A PID that's unlikely to exist should be dead.\n        // because signal handlers run in a separate context and\n48\t/// cannot easily access the Worker's shutdown flag directly.\n49\t#[cfg(unix)]\n50\tstatic GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n51\t\n52\t/// Set the global shutdown flag pointer.\n53\t/// Called by `install_signal_handlers` to register the shutdown flag.\n54\t#[cfg(unix)]\n55\tfn set_global_shutdown_flag(ptr: usize) {\n56\t    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(ptr, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\t/// Clear the global shutdown flag pointer.\n60\t/// Called when the worker is dropped to avoid dangling pointers.\n61\t#[cfg(unix)]\n62\tfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n63\t    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n64\t    LAST_SIGNAL.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\t/// Track the last received signal for diagnostic logging.\n68\t/// AtomicU32 allows lock-free reads/writes from the signal handler.\n69\t#[cfg(unix)]\n70\tstatic LAST_SIGNAL: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);\n71\t\n72\t/// Synchronous signal handler for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n73\t///\n74\t/// This function is called directly by the OS when a signal is received.\n75\t/// It must be async-signal-safe: no allocation, no locking, no I/O.\n76\t/// We set the atomic shutdown flag, record the signal number, and return immediately.\n77\t#[cfg(unix)]\n78\textern \"C\" fn signal_handler(sig: i32) {\n79\t    // SAFETY: The signal handler is only installed after set_global_shutdown_flag\n80\t    // has been called with a valid pointer. The pointer remains valid for the\n81\t    // entire lifetime of the worker process.\n82\t    let ptr = GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.load(AtomicOrdering::SeqCst) as *const AtomicBool;\n83\t    if !ptr.is_null() {\n84\t        // SAFETY: The pointer is valid and points to an AtomicBool that lives\n85\t        // for the entire program duration.\n86\t        unsafe {\n87\t            (*ptr).store(true, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n88\t        }\n89\t        // Record the signal number so the main loop can log it.\n90\t        LAST_SIGNAL.store(sig as u32, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n91\t    }\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// Install synchronous signal handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n95\t///\n96\t/// Uses libc::sigaction to register handlers that set the shutdown flag\n97\t/// immediately when a signal is received. This ensures that signals are\n98\t/// caught even if the tokio runtime hasn't polled async signal tasks yet.\n99\t#[cfg(unix)]\n100\tunsafe fn install_unix_signal_handlers() {\n101\t    use libc::{sigaction, sigemptyset, SA_RESTART, SIGHUP, SIGINT, SIGTERM};\n102\t\n103\t    // Set up the sigaction structure.\n104\t    let mut act: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n105\t    act.sa_sigaction = signal_handler as *const () as usize;\n106\t    // Block all signals during handler execution to prevent re-entrancy issues.\n107\t    sigemptyset(&mut act.sa_mask as *mut libc::sigset_t);\n108\t    // Use SA_RESTART to automatically restart system calls interrupted by signals.\n109\t    act.sa_flags = SA_RESTART;\n110\t\n111\t    // Install handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n112\t    // We ignore errors here - if a handler can't be installed, we'll log a\n113\t    // warning but continue. The async handlers (below) provide a fallback.\n114\t    for &sig in &[SIGTERM, SIGINT, SIGHUP] {\n115\t        let mut old: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n116\t        if sigaction(sig, &act, &mut old) == 0 {\n117\t            tracing::debug!(signal = sig, \"installed synchronous signal handler\");\n118\t        } else {\n119\t            // Log the error but don't fail - the async handlers provide a fallback.\n120\t            tracing::warn!(\n121\t                signal = sig,\n122\t                errno = *libc::__errno_location(),\n123\t                \"failed to install synchronous signal handler\"\n124\t            );\n125\t        }\n126\t    }\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t/// Stub implementations for non-Unix platforms.\n130\t/// These functions are no-ops on platforms where Unix signals are not available.\n131\t#[cfg(not(unix))]\n132\tfn set_global_shutdown_flag(_ptr: usize) {\n133\t    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n134\t}\n135\t\n136\t#[cfg(not(unix))]\n137\tfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n138\t    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n139\t}\n140\t\n141\t/// TTL for race-lost bead exclusions.\n142\t///\n143\t/// After losing a claim race, a bead is excluded from selection for this duration\n144\t/// to prevent infinite loops where the selector returns the same bead repeatedly.\n145\tconst RACE_LOST_EXCLUSION_TTL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);\n146\t\n147\t/// Timeout for HANDLING state watchdog.\n148\t///\n149\t/// If the worker remains in HANDLING state for longer than this duration,\n150\t/// the watchdog thread will force a recovery. This is longer than the\n151\t/// inner timeouts (50s, 60s, 90s) to allow normal recovery to work first.\n152\tconst HANDLING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 120;\n153\t\n154\t/// The NEEDLE worker — owns and drives the full state machine.\n155\tpub struct Worker {\n156\t    config: Config,\n157\t    worker_name: String,\n158\t    store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n159\t    /// Home workspace store — kept for restore after processing a remote bead.\n160\t    home_store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n161\t    telemetry: Telemetry,\n162\t    strands: StrandRunner,\n163\t    claimer: Claimer,\n164\t    prompt_builder: PromptBuilder,\n165\t    dispatcher: Dispatcher,\n166\t    outcome_handler: OutcomeHandler,\n167\t    health: HealthMonitor,\n168\t    registry: Registry,\n169\t    rate_limiter: RateLimiter,\n170\t    mitosis_evaluator: MitosisEvaluator,\n171\t\n172\t    // State machine fields\n173\t    state: WorkerState,\n174\t    current_bead: Option<Bead>,\n175\t    exclusion_set: HashSet<BeadId>,\n176\t    /// Race-lost exclusions with TTL — prevents re-selecting beads that just lost a claim race.\n177\t    /// Each entry is (bead_id, expiration_time). Entries are pruned on access.\n178\t    race_lost_exclusions: Vec<(BeadId, Instant)>,\n179\t    /// Beads that lost a claim race in the current selection cycle.\n180\t    /// These are added to exclusion_set to prevent immediate re-selection.\n181\t    /// Cleared at the start of the next SELECTING cycle.\n182\t    race_lost_this_cycle: HashSet<BeadId>,\n183\t    retry_count: u32,\n184\t    consecutive_race_lost: u32,\n185\t    beads_processed: u64,\n186\t    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n187\t    last_error: Option<anyhow::Error>,\n188\t    boot_time: Option<Instant>,\n189\t\n190\t    // Transient fields — pass data between state handlers within a single cycle.\n191\t    built_prompt: Option<BuiltPrompt>,\n192\t    current_strand: Option<String>,\n193\t    exec_output: Option<(AgentOutcome, bool)>,\n194\t    /// Effort tracking data for the current bead cycle.\n195\t    last_effort: Option<EffortData>,\n196\t    /// HEAD SHA captured just before agent dispatch; used to detect new commits.\n197\t    pre_dispatch_head: Option<String>,\n198\t    /// The workspace of the current bead store — updated when switching to remote.\n199\t    /// Used to ensure heartbeat reports the actual workspace where work is happening.\n200\t    current_workspace: PathBuf,"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"93d29db8-cd8d-4349-b3e0-4d5deb562d0d","uuid":"287ccfe4-aef9-42e6-a243-ef468757b93a","timestamp":"2026-04-26T00:36:44.847Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/worker/mod.rs","content":"//! HotReloadCheck};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Global shutdown flag for signal handlers\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Global pointer to the shutdown flag, used by synchronous signal handlers.\n/// This is necessary because signal handlers run in a separate context and\n/// cannot easily access the Worker's shutdown flag directly.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nstatic GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n\n/// Set the global shutdown flag pointer.\n/// Called by `install_signal_handlers` to register the shutdown flag.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nfn set_global_shutdown_flag(ptr: usize) {\n    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(ptr, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n}\n\n/// Clear the global shutdown flag pointer.\n/// Called when the worker is dropped to avoid dangling pointers.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n    GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n    LAST_SIGNAL.store(0, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n}\n\n/// Track the last received signal for diagnostic logging.\n/// AtomicU32 allows lock-free reads/writes from the signal handler.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nstatic LAST_SIGNAL: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);\n\n/// Synchronous signal handler for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n///\n/// This function is called directly by the OS when a signal is received.\n/// It must be async-signal-safe: no allocation, no locking, no I/O.\n/// We set the atomic shutdown flag, record the signal number, and return immediately.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nextern \"C\" fn signal_handler(sig: i32) {\n    // SAFETY: The signal handler is only installed after set_global_shutdown_flag\n    // has been called with a valid pointer. The pointer remains valid for the\n    // entire lifetime of the worker process.\n    let ptr = GLOBAL_SHUTDOWN_FLAG.load(AtomicOrdering::SeqCst) as *const AtomicBool;\n    if !ptr.is_null() {\n        // SAFETY: The pointer is valid and points to an AtomicBool that lives\n        // for the entire program duration.\n        unsafe {\n            (*ptr).store(true, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n        }\n        // Record the signal number so the main loop can log it.\n        LAST_SIGNAL.store(sig as u32, AtomicOrdering::SeqCst);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Install synchronous signal handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n///\n/// Uses libc::sigaction to register handlers that set the shutdown flag\n/// immediately when a signal is received. This ensures that signals are\n/// caught even if the tokio runtime hasn't polled async signal tasks yet.\n#[cfg(unix)]\nunsafe fn install_unix_signal_handlers() {\n    use libc::{sigaction, sigemptyset, SA_RESTART, SIGHUP, SIGINT, SIGTERM};\n\n    // Set up the sigaction structure.\n    let mut act: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n    act.sa_sigaction = signal_handler as *const () as usize;\n    // Block all signals during handler execution to prevent re-entrancy issues.\n    sigemptyset(&mut act.sa_mask as *mut libc::sigset_t);\n    // Use SA_RESTART to automatically restart system calls interrupted by signals.\n    act.sa_flags = SA_RESTART;\n\n    // Install handlers for SIGTERM, SIGINT, and SIGHUP.\n    // We ignore errors here - if a handler can't be installed, we'll log a\n    // warning but continue. The async handlers (below) provide a fallback.\n    for &sig in &[SIGTERM, SIGINT, SIGHUP] {\n        let mut old: libc::sigaction = std::mem::zeroed();\n        if sigaction(sig, &act, &mut old) == 0 {\n            tracing::debug!(signal = sig, \"installed synchronous signal handler\");\n        } else {\n            // Log the error but don't fail - the async handlers provide a fallback.\n            tracing::warn!(\n                signal = sig,\n                errno = *libc::__errno_location(),\n                \"failed to install synchronous signal handler\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Stub implementations for non-Unix platforms.\n/// These functions are no-ops on platforms where Unix signals are not available.\n#[cfg(not(unix))]\nfn set_global_shutdown_flag(_ptr: usize) {\n    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n}\n\n#[cfg(not(unix))]\nfn clear_global_shutdown_flag() {\n    // No-op on non-Unix platforms\n}\n\n/// TTL for race-lost bead exclusions.\n///\n/// After losing a claim race, a bead is excluded from selection for this duration\n/// to prevent infinite loops where the selector returns the same bead repeatedly.\nconst RACE_LOST_EXCLUSION_TTL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);\n\n/// Timeout for HANDLING state watchdog.\n///\n/// If the worker remains in HANDLING state for longer than this duration,\n/// the watchdog thread will force a recovery. This is longer than the\n/// inner timeouts (50s, 60s, 90s) to allow normal recovery to work first.\nconst HANDLING_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 120;\n\n/// The NEEDLE worker — owns and drives the full state machine.\npub struct Worker {\n    config: Config,\n    worker_name: String,\n    store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n    /// Home workspace store — kept for restore after processing a remote bead.\n    home_store: Arc<dyn BeadStore>,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n    strands: StrandRunner,\n    claimer: Claimer,\n    prompt_builder: PromptBuilder,\n    dispatcher: Dispatcher,\n    outcome_handler: OutcomeHandler,\n    health: HealthMonitor,\n    registry: Registry,\n    rate_limiter: RateLimiter,\n    mitosis_evaluator: MitosisEvaluator,\n\n    // State machine fields\n    state: WorkerState,\n    current_bead: Option<Bead>,\n    exclusion_set: HashSet<BeadId>,\n    /// Race-lost exclusions with TTL — prevents re-selecting beads that just lost a claim race.\n    /// Each entry is (bead_id, expiration_time). Entries are pruned on access.\n    race_lost_exclusions: Vec<(BeadId, Instant)>,\n    /// Beads that lost a claim race in the current selection cycle.\n    /// These are added to exclusion_set to prevent immediate re-selection.\n    /// Cleared at the start of the next SELECTING cycle.\n    race_lost_this_cycle: HashSet<BeadId>,\n    retry_count: u32,\n    consecutive_race_lost: u32,\n    beads_processed: u64,\n    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    last_error: Option<anyhow::Error>,\n    boot_time: Option<Instant>,\n\n    // Transient fields — pass data between state handlers within a single cycle.\n    built_prompt: Option<BuiltPrompt>,\n    current_strand: Option<String>,\n    exec_output: Option<(AgentOutcome, bool)>,\n    /// Effort tracking data for the current bead cycle.\n    last_effort: Option<EffortData>,\n    /// HEAD SHA captured just before agent dispatch; used to detect new commits.\n    pre_dispatch_head: Option<String>,\n    /// The workspace of the current bead store — updated when switching to remote.\n    /// Used to ensure heartbeat reports the actual workspace where work is happening.\n    current_workspace: PathBuf,","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":3308}}}

Called on graceful shutdown (STOPPED) and best-effort on ERRORED.\n209\t    pub fn stop(&mut self) {\n210\t        // Signal the emitter thread to exit.\n211\t        self.shutdown.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n212\t\n213\t        // Join the emitter thread (with a timeout to avoid hanging).\n214\t        if let Some(handle) = self.emitter_handle.take() {\n215\t            // Give the
