self.heartbeat_ttl\n256\t    }\n257\t\n258\t    // ── Reader utilities (used by peer monitoring / Mend strand) ────────────\n259\t\n260\t    /// Read all heartbeat files in the given directory.\n261\t    ///\n262\t    /// Silently skips files that cannot be read or parsed (they may be\n263\t    /// partially written or from a crashed worker).\n264\t    pub fn read_all_heartbeats(dir: &Path) -> Result<Vec<HeartbeatData>> {\n265\t        let mut heartbeats = Vec::new();\n266\t\n267\t        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(dir) {\n268\t            Ok(e) => e,\n269\t            Err(e) if           if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) != Some(\"json\") {\n287\t                continue;\n288\t            }\n289\t\n290\t            match std::fs::read_to_string(&path) {\n291\t }\n315\t            }\n316\t        }\n317\t\n318\t        Ok(heartbeats)\n319\t    }\n320\t\n321\t    /// Check whether a heartbeat is stale (exceeded TTL).\n322\t    pub fn is_stale(heartbeat: .unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO);\n327\t        age > ttl\n328\t    }\n329\t\n330\t    /// Check whether a process with the given PID is alive.\n331\t    ///\n332\t    /// Uses `kill -0` semantics: sends .args([\"-0\", &pid.to_string()])\n337\t            .stdout(std::process::Stdio::null())\n338\t            .stderr(std::process::Stdio::null())\n339\t            .status()\n340\t            .map(|s| ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n378\t\n379\t    /// Write a heartbeat file atomically (write temp, then rename).\n380\t    fn write_heartbeat(&self) -> Result<()> json.as_bytes()).with_context(|| {\n423\t            format!(\n424\t                \"failed to write temp heartbeat file: {}\",\n425\t                tmp_path.display()\n426\t            )\n427\t        })?;\n428\t        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path).with_context(|| {\n429\t            format!(\n430\t                \"failed to rename heartbeat file: {} -> {}\",\n431\t                tmp_path.display(),\n432\t                path.display()\n433\t            )\n434\t        })?;\n435\t\n436\t        Ok(())\n437\t    }\n438\t}\n439\t\n440\timpl Drop for HealthMonitor {\n441\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n442\t        // Best-effort: signal the emitter and clean up the heartbeat file.\n443\t        self.stop();\n444\t    }\n445\t}\n446\t\n447\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n448\t// StalePeer\n449\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n450\t\n451\t/// A peer worker detected as having a stale heartbeat.\n452\t#[derive(Debug)]\n453\tpub struct StalePeer {\n454\t    pub worker_id: String,\n455\t    /// Fully-qualified identity of the peer.\n456\t    pub qualified_id: Option<String>,\n457\t    pub pid: u32,\n458\t    pub pid_alive: bool,\n459\t    pub current_bead: Option<BeadId>,\n460\t    pub last_heartbeat: DateTime<Utc>,\n461\t    pub heartbeat_file: PathBuf,\n462\t}\n463\t\n464\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n465\t// Emitter loop (runs in a dedicated std::thread)\n466\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n467\t\n468\t/// Maximum sleep between heartbeat attempts when backing off on failure.\n469\tconst MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF: Duration = Duration::from_secs(5 * 60);\n470\t\n471\t/// Background emitter loop. Writes heartbeat at each interval.\n472\t///\n473\t/// Circuit breaker: after `max_consecutive_failures` consecutive write failures\n474\t/// the loop sets the shutdown flag and exits so the worker terminates instead of\n475\t/// spinning indefinitely.\n476\t///\n477\t/// Backoff: each consecutive failure doubles the inter-attempt sleep, capped at\n478\t/// [`MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF`].\n479\t#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n480\tfn emitter_loop(\n481\t    shared_state: Arc<Mutex<SharedHeartbeatState>>,\n482\t    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n483\t    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n484\t    worker_id: String,\n485\t    qualified_id: String,\n486\t    workspace: PathBuf,\n487\t    started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n488\t    interval: Duration,\n489\t    max_consecutive_failures: u32,\n490\t) {\n491\t    // Ensure the heartbeat directory exists before entering the write loop so\n492\t    // that workers self-recover if ~/.needle/state/heartbeats/ is deleted.\n493\t    if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(&heartbeat_dir) {\n494\t        tracing::error!(\n495\t            error = %e,\n496\t            dir = %heartbeat_dir.display(),\n497\t            \"failed to create heartbeat directory\"\n498\t        );\n499\t    }\n500\t\n501\t    let mut consecutive_failures: u32 = 0;\n502\t    let mut current_sleep = interval;\n503\t\n504\t    loop {\n505\t        std::thread::sleep(current_sleep);\n506\t\n507\t        if shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {\n508\t            tracing::debug!(worker = %worker_id, \"heartbeat emitter shutting down\");\n509\t            return;\n510\t        }\n511\t\n512\t        let (state, current_bead, beads_processed, current_workspace) = match shared_state.lock() {\n513\t            Ok(guard) => (\n514\t                guard.state.clone(),\n515\t                guard.current_bead.clone(),\n516\t                guard.beads_processed,\n517\t                guard.current_workspace.clone(),\n518\t            ),\n519\t            Err(_) => {\n520\t                // Mutex poisoned — the main thread panicked. Exit.\n521\t                tracing::error!(\n522\t                    worker = %worker_id,\n523\t                    \"shared state mutex poisoned, heartbeat emitter exiting\"\n524\t                );\n525\t                return;\n526\t            }\n527\t        };\n528\t\n529\t        // Use the current bead's workspace if set, otherwise fall back to home workspace.\n530\t        let effective_workspace = current_workspace.unwrap_or_else(|| workspace.clone());\n531\t\n532\t        let data = HeartbeatData {\n533\t            worker_id: worker_id.clone(),\n534\t            qualified_id: qualified_id.clone(),\n535\t            pid: std::process::id(),\n536\t            state,\n537\t            current_bead,\n538\t            workspace: effective_workspace,\n539\t            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n540\t            started_at,\n541\t            beads_processed,\n542\t            session: worker_id.clone(),\n543\t            heartbeat_file: None,\n544\t        };\n545\t\n546\t        let path = heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", qualified_id));\n547\t        let tmp_path = path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n548\t\n549\t        let write_result: anyhow::Result<()> = (|| {\n550\t            let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&data)?;\n551\t            std::fs::write(&tmp_path, json.as_bytes())?;\n552\t            std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path)?;\n553\t            Ok(())\n554\t        })();\n555\t\n556\t        match write_result {\n557\t            Ok(()) => {\n558\t                consecutive_failures = 0;\n559\t                current_sleep = interval;\n560\t            }\n561\t            Err(e) => {\n562\t                consecutive_failures += 1;\n563\t                tracing::error!(\n564\t                    error = %e,\n565\t                    worker = %worker_id,\n566\t                    consecutive_failures,\n567\t                    max = max_consecutive_failures,\n568\t                    \"heartbeat write failed\"\n569\t                );\n570\t                if consecutive_failures >= max_consecutive_failures {\n571\t                    tracing::error!(\n572\t                        worker = %worker_id,\n573\t                        consecutive_failures,\n574\t                        \"heartbeat emitter circuit breaker triggered — worker will shut down\"\n575\t                    );\n576\t                    // Emit a final heartbeat event before shutting down so the\n577\t                    // telemetry log shows the circuit breaker was the cause.\n578\t                    let _ = std::fs::write(\n579\t                        heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}-circuit-breaker.txt\", qualified_id)),\n580\t                        format!(\n581\t                            \"Circuit breaker tripped after {} consecutive heartbeat write failures\\n\\\n582\t                             Worker: {}\\n\\\n583\t                             Qualified ID: {}\\n\\\n584\t                             Last error: {}\\n\\\n585\t                             Timestamp: {}\",\n586\t                            consecutive_failures,\n587\t                            worker_id,\n588\t                            qualified_id,\n589\t                            e,\n590\t                            Utc::now().to_rfc3339()\n591\t                        ),\n592\t                    );\n593\t                    shutdown.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n594\t                    return;\n595\t                }\n596\t                // Exponential backoff to reduce log spam before the circuit breaker fires.\n597\t                current_sleep = current_sleep.saturating_mul(2).min(MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF);\n598\t            }\n599\t        }\n600\t    }\n601\t}\n602\t\n603\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n604\t// Tests\n605\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n606\t\n607\t#[cfg(test)]\n608\tmod tests {\n609\t    use super::*;\n610\t    use std::time::Duration;\n611\t\n612\t .unwrap()\n617\t            .parent()\n618\t            .unwrap()\n619\t            .to_path_buf();\n620\t        config.health.heartbeat_interval_secs = 1;\n621\t        config.health.heartbeat_ttl_secs = 5;\n622\t        config\n623\t    }\n624\t\n625\t    #[tokio::test]\n626\t    async fn heartbeat_file_written_on_start() {\n627\t        let dir = #[tokio::test]\n860\t    async fn detect_stale_peers_excludes_self() {\n861\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n862\t        let hb_dir = );\n872\t\n873\t        // Write a stale heartbeat for ourselves.\n874\t        let hb = HeartbeatData {\n875\t            worker_id: \"self-worker\".to_string(),\n876\t            qualified_id: not detect self as stale peer\");\n895\t    }\n896\t\n897\t    /// Verify that the circuit breaker fires after N consecutive write failures:\n898\t    /// the emitter must set the shutdown flag and return rather than looping forever.\n899\t    #[test]\n900\t    fn emitter_exits_after_consecutive_failures() {\n901\t        use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;\n902\t\n903\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n904\t        let hb_dir = dir.path().join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n905\t        std::fs::create_dir_all(&hb_dir).unwrap();\n906\t\n907\t        // Make the heartbeat directory read-only so every write attempt fails.\n908\t        std::fs::set_permissions(&hb_dir, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o555)).unwrap();\n909\t\n910\t        let shared_state = Arc::new(Mutex::new(SharedHeartbeatState {\n911\t            state: WorkerState::Selecting,\n912\t            current_bead: None,\n913\t            beads_processed: 0,\n914\t            current_workspace: None,\n915\t        }));\n916\t        let shutdown = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));\n917\t\n918\t        let shutdown_clone = shutdown.clone();\n919\t        let shared_state_clone = shared_state.clone();\n920\t        let hb_dir_clone = hb_dir.clone();\n921\t\n922\t        // Use a tiny interval and a low failure threshold so the test completes quickly.\n923\t        let handle = std::thread::spawn(move || {\n924\t            emitter_loop(\n925\t                shared_state_clone,\n926\t                shutdown_clone,\n927\t                hb_dir_clone,\n928\t                \"cb-test\".to_string(),\n929\t                \"claude-cb-test\".to_string(),\n930\t                PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n931\t                Utc::now(),\n932\t                Duration::from_millis(1),\n933\t                3, // trip after 3 consecutive failures\n934\t            );\n935\t        });\n936\t\n937\t        handle.join().expect(\"emitter thread panicked\");\n938\t\n939\t        // The circuit breaker must have set the shutdown flag.\n940\t        assert!(\n941\t            shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst),\n942\t            \"shutdown flag must be set after circuit breaker trips\"\n943\t        );\n944\t\n945\t        // Restore permissions so the tempdir can be cleaned up.\n946\t        let _ = std::fs::set_permissions(&hb_dir, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o755));\n947\t    }\n948\t\n949\t    #[tokio::test]\n950\t    async fn heartbeat_path_uses_qualified_id_not_bare_worker_id() {\n951\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n952\t        let hb_dir = dir.path().join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n953\t\n954\t        // Create two configs with different adapters but the same worker name.\n955\t        let mut config1 = test_config(&hb_dir);\n956\t        config1.agent.default = Health monitoring: heartbeats, stale detection, PID checking.\n//!\n//! Workers emit periodic heartbeats from a dedicated background thread.\n//! Peers read heartbeat files to detect crashed or stuck workers.\n//!\n//! The heartbeat emitter uses `std::thread::spawn` (not tokio) to keep it\n//! independent of the async runtime. The main worker updates shared state\n//! via `Arc<Mutex<SharedHeartbeatState>>`.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `config`, `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\nuse crate::config::Config;\nuse crate::telemetry::Telemetry;\nuse crate::types::{BeadId, WorkerState};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// HeartbeatData — on-disk JSON structure\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Data written to the heartbeat JSON file on disk.\n///\n/// Path: `~/.needle/state/heartbeats/<qualified-id>.json`\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct HeartbeatData {\n    /// Bare NATO name (e.g., \"alpha\", \"foxtrot\").\n    pub worker_id: String,\n    /// Fully-qualified identity: `{adapter}-{worker_id}` (e.g., \"claude-code-glm-5-foxtrot\").\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub qualified_id: String,\n    pub pid: u32,\n    pub state: WorkerState,\n    pub current_bead: Option<BeadId>,\n    pub workspace: PathBuf,\n    pub last_heartbeat: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub beads_processed: u64,\n    pub session: String,\n    /// The filename that produced this heartbeat (set during read, not serialized).\n    #[serde(skip)]\n    pub heartbeat_file: Option<PathBuf>,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// SharedHeartbeatState — updated by worker, read by emitter\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Shared state between the main worker loop and the heartbeat emitter thread.\nstruct SharedHeartbeatState {\n    state: WorkerState,\n    current_bead: Option<BeadId>,\n    beads_processed: u64,\n    /// The workspace of the current bead (updates dynamically during cross-workspace work).\n    current_workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// HealthMonitor\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Health monitor for a single worker.\n///\n/// Owns the background heartbeat emitter thread and provides reader utilities\n/// for peer heartbeat files.\npub struct HealthMonitor {\n    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n    heartbeat_interval: Duration,\n    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n    /// Bare NATO name (e.g., \"alpha\", \"foxtrot\").\n    worker_id: String,\n    /// Fully-qualified identity: `{adapter_slug}-{worker_id}` (e.g., \"claude-code-glm-5-foxtrot\").\n    qualified_id: String,\n    workspace: PathBuf,\n    started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    shared_state: Arc<Mutex<SharedHeartbeatState>>,\n    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    emitter_handle: Option<std::thread::JoinHandle<()>>,\n}\n\nimpl HealthMonitor {\n    /// Create a new health monitor.\n    ///\n    /// Does not start the emitter — call `start_emitter()` after construction.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `config` - Worker configuration\n    /// * `worker_name` - Bare NATO name (e.g., \"alpha\", \"foxtrot\")\n    /// * `_telemetry` - Telemetry emitter (unused, kept for API compatibility)\n    /// * `shutdown` - Optional shared shutdown flag. If provided, the emitter's\n    ///   circuit breaker will set this flag to trigger graceful worker shutdown.\n    ///   If None, a private flag is created (test compatibility).\n    pub fn new(\n        config: Config,\n        worker_name: String,\n        _telemetry: Telemetry,\n        shutdown: Option<Arc<AtomicBool>>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let heartbeat_dir = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let heartbeat_interval = Duration::from_secs(config.health.heartbeat_interval_secs);\n        let heartbeat_ttl = Duration::from_secs(config.health.heartbeat_ttl_secs);\n        let qualified_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", config.agent.default, worker_name);\n\n        HealthMonitor {\n            heartbeat_dir,\n            heartbeat_interval,\n            heartbeat_ttl,\n            worker_id: worker_name,\n            qualified_id,\n            workspace: config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            started_at: Utc::now(),\n            shared_state: Arc::new(Mutex::new(SharedHeartbeatState {\n                state: WorkerState::Booting,\n                current_bead: None,\n                beads_processed: 0,\n                current_workspace: None,\n            })),\n            shutdown: shutdown.unwrap_or_else(|| Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false))),\n            emitter_handle: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background heartbeat emitter thread.\n    ///\n    /// The thread writes a heartbeat JSON file every `heartbeat_interval` until\n    /// `stop()` is called.\n    pub fn start_emitter(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n        // Ensure heartbeat directory exists.\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&self.heartbeat_dir).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\n                \"failed to create heartbeat directory: {}\",\n                self.heartbeat_dir.display()\n            )\n        })?;\n\n        // Write the initial heartbeat immediately.\n        self.write_heartbeat()?;\n\n        let shared_state = self.shared_state.clone();\n        let shutdown = self.shutdown.clone();\n        let heartbeat_dir = self.heartbeat_dir.clone();\n        let worker_id = self.worker_id.clone();\n        let qualified_id = self.qualified_id.clone();\n        let workspace = self.workspace.clone();\n        let started_at = self.started_at;\n        let interval = self.heartbeat_interval;\n\n        let handle = std::thread::Builder::new()\n            .name(format!(\"heartbeat-{}\", self.worker_id))\n            .spawn(move || {\n                emitter_loop(\n                    shared_state,\n                    shutdown,\n                    heartbeat_dir,\n                    worker_id,\n                    qualified_id,\n                    workspace,\n                    started_at,\n                    interval,\n                    10,\n                );\n            })\n            .context(\"failed to spawn heartbeat emitter thread\")?;\n\n        self.emitter_handle = Some(handle);\n        tracing::info!(\n            worker = %self.worker_id,\n            interval_secs = self.heartbeat_interval.as_secs(),\n            \"heartbeat emitter started\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update the worker state visible to the heartbeat emitter.\n    ///\n    /// Called by the worker on every state transition.\n    pub fn update_state(\n        &self,\n        state: &WorkerState,\n        current_bead: Option<&BeadId>,\n        workspace: Option<&Path>,\n    ) {\n        if let Ok(mut guard) = self.shared_state.lock() {\n            guard.state = state.clone();\n            guard.current_bead = current_bead.cloned();\n            guard.current_workspace = workspace.map(|p| p.to_path_buf());\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update the beads_processed count visible to the heartbeat emitter.\n    pub fn update_beads_processed(&self, count: u64) {\n        if let Ok(mut guard) = self.shared_state.lock() {\n            guard.beads_processed = count;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Stop the heartbeat emitter and remove this worker's heartbeat file.\n    ///\n    /// Called on graceful shutdown (STOPPED) and best-effort on ERRORED.\n    pub fn stop(&mut self) {\n        // Signal the emitter thread to exit.\n        self.shutdown.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n\n        // Join the emitter thread (with a timeout to avoid hanging).\n        if let Some(handle) = self.emitter_handle.take() {\n            // Give the thread up to 2x the interval to notice shutdown and exit.\n            let _ = handle.join();\n        }\n\n        // Remove the heartbeat file (best-effort).\n        let path = self.heartbeat_path();\n        if path.exists() {\n            if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    path = %path.display(),\n                    \"failed to remove heartbeat file on shutdown\"\n                );\n            } else {\n                tracing::debug!(path = %path.display(), \"heartbeat file removed\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Path to this worker's heartbeat file.\n    ///\n    /// Keyed by fully-qualified identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`) to prevent\n    /// collisions when workers from different adapter pools share a NATO name.\n    pub fn heartbeat_path(&self) -> PathBuf {\n        self.heartbeat_dir\n            .join(format!(\"{}.json\", self.qualified_id))\n    }\n\n    /// The fully-qualified identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`).\n    pub fn qualified_id(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.qualified_id\n    }\n\n    /// Directory where heartbeat files are stored.\n    pub fn heartbeat_dir(&self) -> &Path {\n        &self.heartbeat_dir\n    }\n\n    /// The configured heartbeat TTL.\n    pub fn heartbeat_ttl(&self) -> Duration {\n        self.heartbeat_ttl\n    }\n\n    // ── Reader utilities (used by peer monitoring / Mend strand) ────────────\n\n    /// Read all heartbeat files in the given directory.\n    ///\n    /// Silently skips files that cannot be read or parsed (they may be\n    /// partially written or from a crashed worker).\n    pub fn read_all_heartbeats(dir: &Path) -> Result<Vec<HeartbeatData>> {\n        let mut heartbeats = Vec::new();\n\n        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(dir) {\n            Ok(e) => e,\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(heartbeats),\n            Err(e) => {\n                return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(\n                    \"failed to read heartbeat directory {}: {}\",\n                    dir.display(),\n                    e\n                ));\n            }\n        };\n\n        for entry in entries {\n            let entry = match entry {\n                Ok(e) => e,\n                Err(_) => continue,\n            };\n\n            let path = entry.path();\n            if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) != Some(\"json\") {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            match std::fs::read_to_string(&path) {\n                Ok(content) => match serde_json::from_str::<HeartbeatData>(&content) {\n                    Ok(mut hb) => {\n                        // Backfill qualified_id for heartbeats written by older versions.\n                        if hb.qualified_id.is_empty() {\n                            hb.qualified_id = hb.worker_id.clone();\n                        }\n                        hb.heartbeat_file = Some(path.clone());\n                        heartbeats.push(hb)\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        tracing::debug!(\n                            path = %path.display(),\n                            error = %e,\n                            \"skipping unparseable heartbeat file\"\n                        );\n                    }\n                },\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::debug!(\n                        path = %path.display(),\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"skipping unreadable heartbeat file\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(heartbeats)\n    }\n\n    /// Check whether a heartbeat is stale (exceeded TTL).\n    pub fn is_stale(heartbeat: &HeartbeatData, ttl: Duration) -> bool {\n        let age = Utc::now()\n            .signed_duration_since(heartbeat.last_heartbeat)\n            .to_std()\n            .unwrap_or(Duration::ZERO);\n        age > ttl\n    }\n\n    /// Check whether a process with the given PID is alive.\n    ///\n    /// Uses `kill -0` semantics: sends signal 0 to check existence without\n    /// actually sending a signal.\n    pub fn check_pid_alive(pid: u32) -> bool {\n        std::process::Command::new(\"kill\")\n            .args([\"-0\", &pid.to_string()])\n            .stdout(std::process::Stdio::null())\n            .stderr(std::process::Stdio::null())\n            .status()\n            .map(|s| s.success())\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Detect peers with stale heartbeats.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a list of stale peers, excluding this worker.\n    pub fn detect_stale_peers(&self) -> Result<Vec<StalePeer>> {\n        let heartbeats = Self::read_all_heartbeats(&self.heartbeat_dir)?;\n        let mut stale = Vec::new();\n\n        for hb in heartbeats {\n            // Skip our own heartbeat.\n            if hb.qualified_id == self.qualified_id {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            if Self::is_stale(&hb, self.heartbeat_ttl) {\n                let pid_alive = Self::check_pid_alive(hb.pid);\n                let hb_file = hb.heartbeat_file.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                    self.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", hb.qualified_id))\n                });\n                stale.push(StalePeer {\n                    worker_id: hb.worker_id.clone(),\n                    qualified_id: Some(hb.qualified_id.clone()),\n                    pid: hb.pid,\n                    pid_alive,\n                    current_bead: hb.current_bead.clone(),\n                    last_heartbeat: hb.last_heartbeat,\n                    heartbeat_file: hb_file,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(stale)\n    }\n\n    // ── Internal ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Write a heartbeat file atomically (write temp, then rename).\n    fn write_heartbeat(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let (state, current_bead, beads_processed, current_workspace) = {\n            let guard = self\n                .shared_state\n                .lock()\n                .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"shared state lock poisoned: {e}\"))?;\n            (\n                guard.state.clone(),\n                guard.current_bead.clone(),\n                guard.beads_processed,\n                guard.current_workspace.clone(),\n            )\n        };\n\n        // Use the current bead's workspace if set, otherwise fall back to home workspace.\n        let effective_workspace = current_workspace.unwrap_or_else(|| self.workspace.clone());\n\n        let data = HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: self.worker_id.clone(),\n            qualified_id: self.qualified_id.clone(),\n            pid: std::process::id(),\n            state,\n            current_bead,\n            workspace: effective_workspace,\n            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n            started_at: self.started_at,\n            beads_processed,\n            session: self.worker_id.clone(),\n            heartbeat_file: None,\n        };\n\n        let path = self.heartbeat_path();\n        let tmp_path = path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n\n        // Auto-create parent directory so that heartbeats self-recover if the\n        // directory is deleted while a worker is running.\n        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to create heartbeat dir: {}\", parent.display()))?;\n        }\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&data).context(\"failed to serialize heartbeat\")?;\n        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, json.as_bytes()).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\n                \"failed to write temp heartbeat file: {}\",\n                tmp_path.display()\n            )\n        })?;\n        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\n                \"failed to rename heartbeat file: {} -> {}\",\n                tmp_path.display(),\n                path.display()\n            )\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Drop for HealthMonitor {\n    fn drop(&mut self) {\n        // Best-effort: signal the emitter and clean up the heartbeat file.\n        self.stop();\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// StalePeer\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A peer worker detected as having a stale heartbeat.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct StalePeer {\n    pub worker_id: String,\n    /// Fully-qualified identity of the peer.\n    pub qualified_id: Option<String>,\n    pub pid: u32,\n    pub pid_alive: bool,\n    pub current_bead: Option<BeadId>,\n    pub last_heartbeat: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub heartbeat_file: PathBuf,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Emitter loop (runs in a dedicated std::thread)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Maximum sleep between heartbeat attempts when backing off on failure.\nconst MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF: Duration = Duration::from_secs(5 * 60);\n\n/// Background emitter loop. Writes heartbeat at each interval.\n///\n/// Circuit breaker: after `max_consecutive_failures` consecutive write failures\n/// the loop sets the shutdown flag and exits so the worker terminates instead of\n/// spinning indefinitely.\n///\n/// Backoff: each consecutive failure doubles the inter-attempt sleep, capped at\n/// [`MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF`].\n#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\nfn emitter_loop(\n    shared_state: Arc<Mutex<SharedHeartbeatState>>,\n    shutdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n    worker_id: String,\n    qualified_id: String,\n    workspace: PathBuf,\n    started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    interval: Duration,\n    max_consecutive_failures: u32,\n) {\n    // Ensure the heartbeat directory exists before entering the write loop so\n    // that workers self-recover if ~/.needle/state/heartbeats/ is deleted.\n    if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(&heartbeat_dir) {\n        tracing::error!(\n            error = %e,\n            dir = %heartbeat_dir.display(),\n            \"failed to create heartbeat directory\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    let mut consecutive_failures: u32 = 0;\n    let mut current_sleep = interval;\n\n    loop {\n        std::thread::sleep(current_sleep);\n\n        if shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {\n            tracing::debug!(worker = %worker_id, \"heartbeat emitter shutting down\");\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let (state, current_bead, beads_processed, current_workspace) = match shared_state.lock() {\n            Ok(guard) => (\n                guard.state.clone(),\n                guard.current_bead.clone(),\n                guard.beads_processed,\n                guard.current_workspace.clone(),\n            ),\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Mutex poisoned — the main thread panicked. Exit.\n                tracing::error!(\n                    worker = %worker_id,\n                    \"shared state mutex poisoned, heartbeat emitter exiting\"\n                );\n                return;\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Use the current bead's workspace if set, otherwise fall back to home workspace.\n        let effective_workspace = current_workspace.unwrap_or_else(|| workspace.clone());\n\n        let data = HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: worker_id.clone(),\n            qualified_id: qualified_id.clone(),\n            pid: std::process::id(),\n            state,\n            current_bead,\n            workspace: effective_workspace,\n            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n            started_at,\n            beads_processed,\n            session: worker_id.clone(),\n            heartbeat_file: None,\n        };\n\n        let path = heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", qualified_id));\n        let tmp_path = path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n\n        let write_result: anyhow::Result<()> = (|| {\n            let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&data)?;\n            std::fs::write(&tmp_path, json.as_bytes())?;\n            std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path)?;\n            Ok(())\n        })();\n\n        match write_result {\n            Ok(()) => {\n                consecutive_failures = 0;\n                current_sleep = interval;\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                consecutive_failures += 1;\n                tracing::error!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    worker = %worker_id,\n                    consecutive_failures,\n                    max = max_consecutive_failures,\n                    \"heartbeat write failed\"\n                );\n                if consecutive_failures >= max_consecutive_failures {\n                    tracing::error!(\n                        worker = %worker_id,\n                        consecutive_failures,\n                        \"heartbeat emitter circuit breaker triggered — worker will shut down\"\n                    );\n                    // Emit a final heartbeat event before shutting down so the\n                    // telemetry log shows the circuit breaker was the cause.\n                    let _ = std::fs::write(\n                        heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}-circuit-breaker.txt\", qualified_id)),\n                        format!(\n                            \"Circuit breaker tripped after {} consecutive heartbeat write failures\\n\\\n                             Worker: {}\\n\\\n                             Qualified ID: {}\\n\\\n                             Last error: {}\\n\\\n                             Timestamp: {}\",\n                            consecutive_failures,\n                            worker_id,\n                            qualified_id,\n                            e,\n                            Utc::now().to_rfc3339()\n                        ),\n                    );\n                    shutdown.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);\n                    return;\n                }\n                // Exponential backoff to reduce log spam before the circuit breaker fires.\n                current_sleep = current_sleep.saturating_mul(2).min(MAX_HEARTBEAT_BACKOFF);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Tests\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use std::time::Duration;\n\n    fn test_config(heartbeat_dir: &Path) -> Config {\n        let mut config = Config::default();\n        config.workspace.home = heartbeat_dir\n            .parent()\n            .unwrap()\n            .parent()\n            .unwrap()\n            .to_path_buf();\n        config.health.heartbeat_interval_secs = 1;\n        config.health.heartbeat_ttl_secs = 5;\n        config\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn heartbeat_file_written_on_start() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path().join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let config = test_config(&hb_dir);\n        let mut monitor = HealthMonitor::new(\n            config,\n            \"test-worker\".to_string(),\n            Telemetry::new(\"test\".to_string()),\n is_stale_detects_old_heartbeats() {\n        let mut hb = HeartbeatData {\n            worker_id: \"test\".to_string(),\n            qualified_id: \"claude-test\".to_string(),\n            pid: 1,\n            state: WorkerState::Selecting,\n            current_bead: None,\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n            last_heartbeat: Utc::now(),\n            started_at: assert!(HealthMonitor::is_stale(&hb, Duration::from_secs(300)));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn check_pid_alive_current_process() {\n        // Our own PID should be alive.\n \"atomic-test\".to_string(),\n            Telemetry::new(\"test\".to_string()),\n            None,\n        );\n\n        monitor.start_emitter().unwrap();\n\n        // Read the heartbeat file multiple times while it's being updated.\n        for _ in 0..10 {\n            let path = monitor.heartbeat_path();\n            if path.exists() {\n                let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&path).unwrap();\n                // Should always be valid JSON (never a partial write).\n                let result: Result<HeartbeatData, _> = content\n                );\n            }\n            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100));\n        }\n\n        monitor.stop();\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn heartbeat_data_roundtrip() assert!(stale.is_empty(), \"should not detect self as stale peer\");\n    }\n\n    /// Verify that the circuit breaker fires after N consecutive write failures:\n    /// the emitter must set the shutdown flag and return rather than looping forever.\n    #[test]\n    fn emitter_exits_after_consecutive_failures() {\n        use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;\n\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir = dir.path().join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&hb_dir).unwrap();\n\n        // Make the heartbeat directory read-only so every write attempt fails.\n        std::fs::set_permissions(&hb_dir, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o555)).unwrap();\n\n        let shared_state = Arc::new(Mutex::new(SharedHeartbeatState {\n            state: WorkerState::Selecting,\n            current_bead: None,\n            beads_processed: 0,\n            current_workspace: None,\n        }));\n        let shutdown = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));\n\n        let shutdown_clone = shutdown.clone();\n        let shared_state_clone = shared_state.clone();\n        let hb_dir_clone = hb_dir.clone();\n\n        // Use a tiny interval and a low failure threshold so the test completes quickly.\n        let handle = std::thread::spawn(move || {\n            emitter_loop(\n                shared_state_clone,\n                shutdown_clone,\n                hb_dir_clone,\n                \"cb-test\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-cb-test\".to_string(),\n                PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n                Utc::now(),\n                Duration::from_millis(1),\n                3, // trip after 3 consecutive failures\n            );\n        });\n\n        handle.join().expect(\"emitter thread panicked\");\n\n        // The circuit breaker must have set the shutdown flag.\n        assert!(\n            shutdown.load(Ordering::SeqCst),\n            \"shutdown flag must be set after circuit breaker trips\"\n        );\n\n        // Restore permissions so the tempdir can be cleaned up.\n        let _ = std::fs::set_permissions(&hb_dir, std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o755));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn heartbeat_path_uses_qualified_id_not_bare_worker_id() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n   config1.agent.default = \"claude-code-glm-5\".to_string();\n\n        let mut config2 = test_config(&hb_dir);\n        config2.agent.default = \"claude-code-glm-4_7\".to_string();\n\n        // Telemetry::new(\"test\".to_string()),\n            None,\n        );\n\n        // Verify that heartbeat paths are different (keyed by qualified ID).\n        let path1 = monitor1.heartbeat_path();\n should use qualified ID\"\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            path2,\n            hb_dir.join(\"claude-code-glm-4_7-foxtrot.json\"),\n            \"second monitor's heartbeat path should use qualified ID\"\n        );\n        assert_ne!(\n            path1, path2,\n            \"heartbeat paths must be different for same worker name across adapters\"\n        );\n\n        // Verify that \"claude-code-glm-4_7-foxtrot\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn heartbeat_files_dont_collide_across_adapter_pools() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let hb_dir Strand 2 in the waterfall. Runs after Pluck finds no work. Cleans up\n4\t//! stale claims, orphaned locks, broken dependency links, and database\n5\t//! corruption. Depends on: `bead_store`, `config`, `health`, `peer`, `registry`,\n9\t//!             `telemetry`, `types`, `trace`.\n10\t\n11\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n12\tuse std::time::{Duration, SystemTime};\n13\t\n14\tuse anyhow::Result;\n15\tuse chrono::Utc;\n16\t\n17\tuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\n18\tuse crate::config::{LimitsConfig, MendConfig};\n19\tuse crate::health::HealthMonitor;\n20\tuse crate::learning::LearningsFile;\n21\tuse crate::peer::PeerMonitor;\n22\tuse crate::registry::Registry;\n23\tuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\n24\tuse crate::trace::cleanup_traces;\n25\tuse crate::types::{BeadStatus, StrandError, StrandResult};\n26\t\n27\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n28\t// Store-scoped orphan cleanup (shared between Mend and Explore)\n29\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n30\t\n31\t/// Scan all in-progress beads in a store and release any whose assignee does\n32\t/// not correspond to a live worker. This catches beads orphaned by workers\n33\t/// that died without leaving a heartbeat file (or whose heartbeat was already\n34\t/// cleaned up).\n35\t///\n36\t/// This is a store-scoped function that can be called against any BeadStore,\n37\t/// not just the home workspace. Used by both MendStrand (for home) and\n38\t/// ExploreStrand (for remote workspaces).\n39\t///\n40\t/// # Arguments\n41\t/// * `store` - The bead store to scan (can be home or remote)\n42\t/// * `registry` - Worker registry for live worker lookup\n43\t/// * `telemetry` - Telemetry emitter for orphan release events\n44\t/// * `qualified_id` - This worker's fully-qualified identity (excluded from orphan detection)\n45\t///\n46\t/// # Returns\n47\t/// * `Ok(u32)` - Number of orphans released\n48\t/// * `Err(anyhow::Error)` - Store read failure\n49\tpub async fn cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n50\t    store: &dyn BeadStore,\n51\t    registry: &Registry,\n52\t    telemetry: &Telemetry,\n53\t    qualified_id: &str,\n54\t) -> Result<u32> {\n55\t    let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n56\t    let workers = registry.list()?;\n57\t\n58\t    // Build a set of fully-qualified worker IDs for registered, alive workers.\n59\t    let live_worker_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = workers\n60\t        .iter()\n61\t        .filter(|w| HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(w.pid))\n62\t        .map(|w| w.id.clone())\n63\t        .collect();\n64\t\n65\t    let mut released = 0u32;\n66\t\n67\t    for bead in &all_beads {\n68\t        if bead.status != BeadStatus::InProgress {\n69\t            continue;\n70\t        }\n71\t\n72\t        let assignee = match &bead.assignee {\n73\t            Some(a) if !a.is_empty() => a,\n74\t            _ => continue,\n75\t        };\n76\t\n77\t        // Skip if the assignee matches our own qualified identity (we're running).\n78\t        if assignee == qualified_id {\n79\t            continue;\n80\t        }\n81\t\n82\t        // Skip if the assignee matches a registered, alive worker.\n83\t        // Workers register with fully-qualified IDs ({adapter}-{worker_id}),\n84\t        // so this comparison prevents collisions when workers from different\n85\t        // adapter pools share a NATO name.\n86\t        if live_worker_ids.contains(assignee.as_str()) {\n87\t            continue;\n88\t        }\n89\t\n90\t        // Orphaned: assignee is not a live registered worker. Release it.\n91\t        tracing::info!(\n92\t            bead_id = %bead.id,\n93\t            assignee = %assignee,\n94\t            workspace = %bead.workspace.display(),\n95\t            \"releasing orphaned in-progress bead (assignee has no live worker)\"\n96\t        );\n97\t\n98\t        match store.release(&bead.id).await {\n99\t            Ok(()) => {\n100\t                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::BeadReleased {\n101\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n102\t                    reason: format!(\"orphaned: assignee {} has no live worker\", assignee),\n103\t                });\n104\t                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n105\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n106\t                    peer_worker: assignee.clone(),\n107\t                });\n108\t                released += 1;\n109\t            }\n110\t            Err(e) => {\n111\t                tracing::warn!(\n112\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n113\t                    error = %e,\n114\t                    \"failed to release orphaned in-progress bead\"\n115\t                );\n116\t                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendBeadReleaseFailed {\n117\t                    bead_id: bead.id.to_string(),\n118\t                    assignee: assignee.clone(),\n119\t                    error: e.to_string(),\n120\t                });\n121\t            }\n122\t        }\n123\t    }\n124\t\n125\t    Ok(released)\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\t/// Summary of work performed during one Mend evaluation cycle.\n129\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n130\tstruct MendSummary {\n131\t    beads_released: u32,\n132\t    locks_removed: u32,\n133\t    deps_cleaned: u32,\n134\t    db_repaired: bool,\n135\t    db_rebuilt: bool,\n136\t    agent_logs_cleaned: u32,\n137\t    traces_pruned: u32,\n138\t    traces_cleaned: u32,\n139\t    learnings_pruned: u32,\n140\t    learnings_consolidated: u32,\n141\t    orphaned_heartbeats_removed: u32,\n142\t    workers_deregistered: u32,\n143\t    idle_workers_flagged: u32,\n144\t    rate_limits_cleaned: u32,\n145\t    rate_limit_providers_removed: u32,\n146\t    rate_limit_providers_reset: u32,\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\timpl MendSummary {\n150\t    /// Whether mend performed work that changes bead store state.\n151\t    ///\n152\t    /// Only operations that add or release claimable beads should return true.\n153\t    /// A strand MUST return `WorkCreated` only when it inserts a claimable bead\n154\t    /// into the store that the waterfall will re-scan. No-op cleanup (pruning\n155\t    /// traces, removing stale locks, repairing DB) should return `NoWork`.\n156\t    ///\n157\t    /// Operations that return `WorkCreated`:\n158\t    /// - `beads_released > 0`: Orphaned beads were released back to Open status.\n159\t    /// - `deps_cleaned > 0`: Stale dependency links were removed (beads become claimable).\n160\t    ///\n161\t    /// Operations that return `NoWork` (maintenance, not work creation):\n162\t    /// - `locks_removed > 0`: Lock file cleanup (doesn't add beads to queue).\n163\t    /// - `db_repaired`: Doctor repair fixed index corruption (doesn't add beads).\n164\t    /// - `db_rebuilt`: Full rebuild from JSONL (doesn't add beads).\n165\t    /// - `traces_pruned`, `agent_logs_cleaned`, `learnings_*`: File cleanup.\n166\t    ///\n167\t    /// A `WorkCreated` return must be paired with a telemetry event identifying\n168\t    /// the created bead(s) so operators can see what the restart is chasing.\n169\t    fn did_work(&self) -> bool {\n170\t        // Bead release and dependency removal add claimable items to the queue.\n171\t        // Lock removal, DB repair, and DB rebuild are maintenance operations\n172\t        // that don't create new work and must not trigger a waterfall restart.\n173\t        self.beads_released > 0 || self.deps_cleaned > 0\n174\t    }\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\t/// The Mend strand — maintenance and self-healing.\n178\tpub struct MendStrand {\n179\t    config: MendConfig,\n180\t    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n181\t    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n182\t    lock_dir: from different adapter pools share a NATO name.\n186\t    qualified_id: String,\n187\t    registry: Registry,\n188\t    telemetry: Telemetry,\n189\t    log_dir: PathBuf,\n190\t    retention_days: Base state directory (contains `rate_limits/` subdirectory).\n197\t    state_dir: PathBuf,\n198\t    /// Provider/model limits configuration for rate limiter cleanup.\n199\t    limits_config: `/tmp`)\n209\t    /// - `qualified_id`: fully-qualified worker identity ({adapter}-{worker_id})\n210\t    /// - `registry`: worker state registry\n211\t    /// - `telemetry`: telemetry emitter\n212\t    /// - `log_dir`: directory where agent log files live\n213\t    /// - `retention_days`: number of days to retain agent log files (0 = disabled)\n214\t    /// - `traces_dir`: directory where trace files live (`.beads/traces`)\n215\t    /// - `trace_retention_failed_days`: retention days for failed bead traces\n216\t    /// - `trace_retention_success_days`: retention days for successful bead traces\n217\t    /// - `workspace`: workspace root path for learning consolidation\n218\t    /// - `max_learnings`: maximum number of learning entries before consolidation\n219\t    /// - `state_dir`: #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n222\t    pub fn new(\n223\t        config: MendConfig,\n224\t        heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n225\t        heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n226\t        lock_dir: Mend strand: maintenance and self-healing.\n//!\n//! Strand 2 in the waterfall. Runs after Pluck finds no work. Cleans up\n//! stale claims, orphaned locks, broken dependency links, and database\n//! corruption. If any cleanup is performed, returns `WorkCreated` so the\n//! waterfall restarts from Pluck (released beads may now be claimable).\n//!\n//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `config`, `health`, `peer`, `registry`,\n//!             `telemetry`, `types`, `trace`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::time::{Duration, SystemTime};\n\nuse anyhow::Result;\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::config::{LimitsConfig, MendConfig};\nuse crate::health::HealthMonitor;\nuse crate::learning::LearningsFile;\nuse crate::peer::PeerMonitor;\nuse crate::registry::Registry;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::trace::cleanup_traces;\nuse crate::types::{BeadStatus, StrandError, StrandResult};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Store-scoped orphan cleanup (shared between Mend and Explore)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Scan all in-progress beads in a store and release any whose assignee does\n/// not correspond to a live worker. This catches beads orphaned by workers\n/// that died without leaving a heartbeat file (or whose heartbeat was already\n/// cleaned up).\n///\n/// This is a store-scoped function that can be called against any BeadStore,\n/// not just the home workspace. Used by both MendStrand (for home) and\n/// ExploreStrand (for remote workspaces).\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `store` - The bead store to scan (can be home or remote)\n/// * `registry` - Worker registry for live worker lookup\n/// * `telemetry` - Telemetry emitter for orphan release events\n/// * `qualified_id` - This worker's fully-qualified identity (excluded from orphan detection)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(u32)` - Number of orphans released\n/// * `Err(anyhow::Error)` - Store read failure\npub async fn cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n    store: &dyn BeadStore,\n    registry: &Registry,\n    telemetry: &Telemetry,\n    qualified_id: &str,\n) -> Result<u32> {\n    let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n    let workers = registry.list()?;\n\n    // Build a set of fully-qualified worker IDs for registered, alive workers.\n    let live_worker_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = workers\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|w| HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(w.pid))\n        .map(|w| w.id.clone())\n        .collect();\n\n    let mut released = 0u32;\n\n    for bead in &all_beads {\n        if bead.status != BeadStatus::InProgress {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let assignee = match &bead.assignee {\n            Some(a) if !a.is_empty() => a,\n            _ => continue,\n        };\n\n        // Skip if the assignee matches our own qualified identity (we're running).\n        if assignee == qualified_id {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Skip if the assignee matches a registered, alive worker.\n        // Workers register with fully-qualified IDs ({adapter}-{worker_id}),\n        // so this comparison prevents collisions when workers from different\n        // adapter pools share a NATO name.\n        if live_worker_ids.contains(assignee.as_str()) {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Orphaned: assignee is not a live registered worker. Release it.\n        tracing::info!(\n            bead_id = %bead.id,\n            assignee = %assignee,\n            workspace = %bead.workspace.display(),\n            \"releasing orphaned in-progress bead (assignee has no live worker)\"\n        );\n\n        match store.release(&bead.id).await {\n            Ok(()) => {\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::BeadReleased {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    reason: format!(\"orphaned: assignee {} has no live worker\", assignee),\n                });\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    peer_worker: assignee.clone(),\n                });\n                released += 1;\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to release orphaned in-progress bead\"\n                );\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendBeadReleaseFailed {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.to_string(),\n                    assignee: assignee.clone(),\n                    error: e.to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(released)\n}\n\n/// Summary of work performed during one Mend evaluation cycle.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct MendSummary {\n    beads_released: u32,\n    locks_removed: u32,\n    deps_cleaned: u32,\n    db_repaired: bool,\n    db_rebuilt: bool,\n    agent_logs_cleaned: u32,\n    traces_pruned: u32,\n    traces_cleaned: u32,\n    learnings_pruned: u32,\n    learnings_consolidated: u32,\n    orphaned_heartbeats_removed: u32,\n    workers_deregistered: u32,\n    idle_workers_flagged: u32,\n    rate_limits_cleaned: u32,\n    rate_limit_providers_removed: u32,\n    rate_limit_providers_reset: u32,\n}\n\nimpl MendSummary {\n    /// Whether mend performed work that changes bead store state.\n    ///\n    /// Only operations that add or release claimable beads should return true.\n    /// A strand MUST return `WorkCreated` only when it inserts a claimable bead\n    /// into the store that the waterfall will re-scan. No-op cleanup (pruning\n    /// traces, removing stale locks, repairing DB) should return `NoWork`.\n    ///\n    /// Operations that return `WorkCreated`:\n    /// - `beads_released > 0`: Orphaned beads were released back to Open status.\n    /// - `deps_cleaned > 0`: Stale dependency links were removed (beads become claimable).\n    ///\n    /// Operations that return `NoWork` (maintenance, not work creation):\n    /// - `locks_removed > 0`: Lock file cleanup (doesn't add beads to queue).\n    /// - `db_repaired`: Doctor repair fixed index corruption (doesn't add beads).\n    /// - `db_rebuilt`: Full rebuild from JSONL (doesn't add beads).\n    /// - `traces_pruned`, `agent_logs_cleaned`, `learnings_*`: File cleanup.\n    ///\n    /// A `WorkCreated` return must be paired with a telemetry event identifying\n    /// the created bead(s) so operators can see what the restart is chasing.\n    fn did_work(&self) -> bool {\n        // Bead release and dependency removal add claimable items to the queue.\n        // Lock removal, DB repair, and DB rebuild are maintenance operations\n        // that don't create new work and must not trigger a waterfall restart.\n        self.beads_released > 0 || self.deps_cleaned > 0\n    }\n}\n\n/// The Mend strand — maintenance and self-healing.\npub struct MendStrand {\n    config: MendConfig,\n    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n    lock_dir: PathBuf,\n    /// Fully-qualified worker identity ({adapter}-{worker_id}).\n    /// Used for heartbeat file lookups and registry comparisons to prevent\n    /// collisions when workers from different adapter pools share a NATO name.\n    qualified_id: String,\n    registry: Registry,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n    log_dir: PathBuf,\n    retention_days: u32,\n    traces_dir: PathBuf,\n    trace_retention_failed_days: u32,\n    trace_retention_success_days: u32,\n    workspace: PathBuf,\n    max_learnings: usize,\n    /// Base state directory (contains `rate_limits/` subdirectory).\n    state_dir: PathBuf,\n    /// Provider/model limits configuration for rate limiter cleanup.\n    limits_config: LimitsConfig,\n}\n\nimpl MendStrand {\n    /// Create a new MendStrand.\n    ///\n    /// - `config`: mend strand configuration\n    /// - `heartbeat_dir`: path to `~/.needle/state/heartbeats/`\n    /// - `heartbeat_ttl`: how long before a heartbeat is considered stale\n    /// - `lock_dir`: directory where claim lock files live (default: `/tmp`)\n    /// - `qualified_id`: fully-qualified worker identity ({adapter}-{worker_id})\n    /// - `registry`: worker state registry\n    /// - `telemetry`: telemetry emitter\n    /// - `log_dir`: directory where agent log files live\n    /// - `retention_days`: number of days to retain agent log files (0 = disabled)\n    /// - `traces_dir`: directory where trace files live (`.beads/traces`)\n    /// - `trace_retention_failed_days`: retention days for failed bead traces\n    /// - `trace_retention_success_days`: retention days for successful bead traces\n    /// - `workspace`: workspace root path for learning consolidation\n    /// - `max_learnings`: maximum number of learning entries before consolidation\n    /// - `state_dir`: base state directory (contains `rate_limits/` subdirectory)\n    /// - `limits_config`: provider/model limits configuration for rate limiter cleanup\n    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n    pub fn new(\n        config: MendConfig,\n        heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n        heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n        lock_dir: PathBuf,\n        qualified_id: String,\n        registry: Registry,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n        log_dir: PathBuf,\n        retention_days: u32,\n        traces_dir: PathBuf,\n        trace_retention_failed_days: u32,\n        trace_retention_success_days: u32,\n        workspace: PathBuf,\n        max_learnings: usize,\n        state_dir: PathBuf,\n        limits_config: LimitsConfig,\n    ) -> Self {\n        MendStrand {\n            config,\n            heartbeat_dir,\n            heartbeat_ttl,\n            lock_dir,\n            qualified_id,\n            registry,\n            telemetry,\n            log_dir,\n            retention_days,\n            traces_dir,\n            trace_retention_failed_days,\n            trace_retention_success_days,\n            workspace,\n            max_learnings,\n            state_dir,\n            limits_config,\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 1: Stale claim cleanup via peer monitoring ──────────────────────\n\n    /// Check for crashed workers and release their orphaned beads.\n    async fn cleanup_stale_claims(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let peer_monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            self.heartbeat_dir.clone(),\n            self.heartbeat_ttl,\n            self.qualified_id.clone(),\n            store,\n            &self.registry,\n            self.telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let peer_result = peer_monitor.check_peers().await?;\n        summary.beads_released += peer_result.beads_released;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 1.5: Orphaned in-progress bead recovery ────────────────────────\n\n    /// Scan all in-progress beads and release any whose continue;\n363\t            }\n364\t\n365\t            // Check if the heartbeat is stale (older than TTL).\n366\t            // Only remove stale heartbeats to avoid deleting recently orphaned };\n381\t\n382\t            match std::fs::remove_file(&heartbeat_path) {\n383\t                Ok(()) => {\n384\t                    tracing::info!(\n385\t                        worker_id = process.\n416\t    ///\n417\t    /// This function immediately cleans up lock files whose holding process\n418\t    /// has died, without waiting for an age-based timeout. The flock(2) lock\n419\t    /// is automatically released by the kernel when the holder process dies,\n420\t    /// so try_acquire_flock() will succeed immediately and we can remove the\n421\t    /// stale metadata file.\n422\t    ///\n423\t    /// Lock files older than lock_ttl_secs are also cleaned up as a fallback\n424\t    /// to handle edge cases where the flock probe might fail.\n425\t    fn cleanup_orphaned_locks(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n426\t        let lock_ttl = Duration::from_secs(self.config.lock_ttl_secs);\n427\t\n428\t        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.lock_dir) {\n429\t            Ok(e) => e,\n430\t            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n431\t            Err(e) => {\n432\t                tracing::warn!(\n433\t                    dir = %self.lock_dir.display(),\n434\t                    error = %e,\n435\t                    \"failed to read lock directory\"\n436\t                );\n437\t                return Ok(());\n438\t            }\n439\t        };\n440\t\n441\t        for entry in entries {\n442\t            let entry = match entry {\n443\t                Ok(e) => e,\n444\t                Err(_) => continue,\n445\t            };\n446\t\n447\t            let path = entry.path();\n448\t            let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n449\t                Some(n) => n,\n450\t                None => continue,\n451\t            };\n452\t\n453\t            // Only consider needle claim lock files.\n454\t            if !name.starts_with(\"needle-claim-\") || !name.ends_with(\".lock\") {\n455\t                continue;\n456\t            }\n457\t\n458\t            // Check file age for logging/fallback cleanup.\n459\t            let age = match file_age(&path) {\n460\t                Some(age) => age,\n461\t                None => continue,\n462\t            };\n463\t\n464\t            // Try to acquire flock (non-blocking). If we can acquire it,\n465\t            // no one is holding it — safe to delete immediately.\n466\t            //\n467\t            // The kernel releases flocks when the holder process dies, so this\n468\t            // probe succeeds immediately for dead PIDs regardless of file age.\n469\t            match try_acquire_flock(&path) {\n470\t                Ok(Some(_file)) => {\n471\t                    // Lock acquired — no one holds it. Remove the file.\n472\t                    let age_secs = age.as_secs();\n473\t                    if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n474\t                        tracing::warn!(\n475\t                            path = %path.display(),\n476\t                            error = %e,\n477\t                            \"failed to remove orphaned lock file\"\n478\t                        );\n479\t                        let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendLockRemoveFailed {\n480\t                            lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n481\t                            error: e.to_string(),\n482\t                        });\n483\t                        continue;\n484\t                    }\n485\t\n486\t                    tracing::info!(\n487\t                        path = %path.display(),\n488\t                        age_secs,\n489\t                        \"removed orphaned lock file\"\n490\t                    );\n491\t\n492\t                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendOrphanedLockRemoved {\n493\t                        lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n494\t                        age_secs,\n495\t                    });\n496\t\n497\t                    summary.locks_removed += 1;\n498\t                }\n499\t                Ok(None) => {\n500\t                    // Lock is actively held — skip.\n501\t                    // Only log if the file is old; otherwise it's just a normal active lock.\n502\t                    if age > lock_ttl {\n503\t                        tracing::debug!(\n504\t                            path = %path.display(),\n505\t                            age_secs = age.as_secs(),\n506\t                            \"lock file is old but actively held, skipping\"\n507\t                        );\n508\t                    }\n509\t                }\n510\t                Err(e) => {\n511\t                    tracing::debug!(\n512\t                        path = %path.display(),\n513\t                        error = %e,\n514\t                        \"failed to probe lock file\"\n515\t                    );\n516\t                }\n517\t            }\n518\t        }\n519\t\n520\t  /// Remove claim lock files that are not actively held by any process.\n    ///\n    /// This function immediately cleans up lock files whose holding process\n    /// has died, without waiting for an age-based timeout. The flock(2) lock\n    /// is automatically released by the kernel when the holder process dies,\n    /// so try_acquire_flock() will succeed immediately and we can remove the\n    /// stale metadata file.\n    ///\n    /// Lock files older than lock_ttl_secs are also cleaned up as a fallback\n    /// to handle edge cases where the flock probe might fail.\n    fn cleanup_orphaned_locks(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        let lock_ttl = Duration::from_secs(self.config.lock_ttl_secs);\n\n        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.lock_dir) {\n            Ok(e) => e,\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    dir = %self.lock_dir.display(),\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to read lock directory\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        for entry in entries {\n            let entry = match entry {\n                Ok(e) => e,\n                Err(_) => continue,\n            };\n\n            let path = entry.path();\n            let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n                Some(n) => n,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            // Only consider needle claim lock files.\n            if !name.starts_with(\"needle-claim-\") || !name.ends_with(\".lock\") {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check file age for logging/fallback cleanup.\n            let age = match file_age(&path) {\n                Some(age) => age,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            // Try to acquire flock (non-blocking). If we can acquire it,\n            // no one is holding it — safe to delete immediately.\n            //\n            // The kernel releases flocks when the holder process dies, so this\n            // probe succeeds immediately for dead PIDs regardless of file age.\n            match try_acquire_flock(&path) {\n                Ok(Some(_file)) => {\n                    // Lock acquired — no one holds it. Remove the file.\n                    let age_secs = age.as_secs();\n                    if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            path = %path.display(),\n                            error = %e,\n                            \"failed to remove orphaned lock file\"\n                        );\n                        let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendLockRemoveFailed {\n                            lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n                            error: e.to_string(),\n                        });\n                        continue;\n                    }\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        path = %path.display(),\n                        age_secs,\n                        \"removed orphaned lock file\"\n                    );\n\n                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendOrphanedLockRemoved {\n                        lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n                        age_secs,\n                    });\n\n                    summary.locks_removed += 1;\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    // Lock is actively held — skip.\n                    // Only log if the file is old; otherwise it's just a normal active lock.\n                    if age > lock_ttl {\n skipping\"\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::debug!(\n                        path = %path.display(),\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"failed to probe lock file\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step The MockBeadStore's `release()` method always returns `Ok(())` and increments the release_count. This means when PeerMonitor tries to release the bead from the stale heartbeat, it succeeds even though there's no such bead in the store.\n\nThe issue is:\n1. The test creates a stale heartbeat with `current_bead: Some(\"nd-ghost\")`\n2. The peer monitor runs and tries to release that bead\n3. The MockBeadStore's `release()` returns Ok even though the bead doesn't exist in `all_beads`\n4. The summary's `beads_released` counter gets incremented\n5. `did_work()` returns true because `beads_released > 0`\n6. The function returns `WorkCreated`\n\nThe fix should be one of:\n1. Make the MockBeadStore's `release()` method check if the bead exists in `all_beads`\n2. Change the test to not create heartbeats with beads\n\nLooking at the test more closely:\n```rust\nwrite_heartbeat(\n    hb_dir.path(),\n    &make_stale_heartbeat(\"ghost-worker\", 99_999_999, Some(\"nd-ghost\")),\n);\n```\n\nThe `make_stale_heartbeat` creates a heartbeat with a bead. The test doesn't add this bead to the store, so the release should be a no-op (or error).\n\nBut the MockBeadStore's `release` implementation just increments the counter unconditionally. This is different from the peer module's MockBeadStore which does the same thing.\n\nThe real issue is that the peer monitor's `handle_crashed_peer` method:\n```rust\nif let Some(ref bead_id) = peer.current_bead {\n    match self.store.release(bead_id).await {\n        Ok(()) => {\n            // ...\n            bead_released = true;\n        }\n        // ...\n    }\n}\n```\n\nIt sets `bead_released = true` whenever `release()` returns Ok. But in the test, we're calling `release()` on a bead that doesn't exist, and the mock returns Ok.\n\n\nThe best fix is to update the MockBeadStore's `release()` method to verify the bead actually exists in `all_beads` before returning Ok. This way, attempting to release a non-existent bead will fail, matching how a real store would behave.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"93d29db8-cd8d-4349-b3e0-4d5deb562d0d","uuid":"6a63d020-781e-40f5-bd01-5988a555e958"}
